    52вҶ’                          в”Ӯ                     в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ
    53вҶ’                    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    54вҶ’                    в”Ӯ              Shared Services (via UDS)               в”Ӯ
    55вҶ’                    в”Ӯ  Auth в”Ӯ Cache в”Ӯ MCP Router в”Ӯ Key Vault в”Ӯ Tool Reg    в”Ӯ
    56вҶ’                    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
    57вҶ’```
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’### 3.2 Component Responsibilities
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’| Component | Role |
    62вҶ’|-----------|------|
    63вҶ’| `forge` CLI | User interaction, plan/generate/run commands, talks to daemon via gRPC |
    64вҶ’| `forge-runtime` daemon | Agent lifecycle, Nix evaluation, service routing, approval gating, state persistence |
    65вҶ’| Agent processes | Claude CLI instances running inside Nix-materialized, namespace-isolated environments |
    66вҶ’| Shared services | Long-lived processes managed by the daemon, exposed to agents via Unix domain sockets |
    67вҶ’
    68вҶ’### 3.3 Key Design Decisions
    69вҶ’
    70вҶ’- CLI and daemon communicate over **gRPC on a Unix domain socket**. Socket path resolution: `$FORGE_SOCKET_PATH` (override) вҶ’ `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/forge.sock` (Linux) вҶ’ `$TMPDIR/forge-$UID/forge.sock` (macOS). gRPC provides streaming (live agent output) and strong typing (protobuf schemas).
    71вҶ’- The daemon is a **single Rust binary** (`forge-runtime`) using tokio.
    72вҶ’- Agents are **not containers by default** вҖ” they run in Linux namespaces (bubblewrap) with Nix-provided environments for sub-second spawning.
    73вҶ’- The existing orchestration logic (DAG scheduler, phase runner, reviews, Factory) stays in the CLI but delegates agent execution to the daemon.
    74вҶ’- **macOS runtime**: See Section 4.6 for macOS-specific runtime details.
    75вҶ’- **Nix is optional**: Agents can run in "host mode" (current behavior, no Nix) or "profile mode" (Nix-defined environments). See Section 4.7.
    76вҶ’- **Version negotiation**: CLI and daemon may be updated independently. See Section 7.5.
    77вҶ’
    78вҶ’## 4. Agent Lifecycle & Nix Environment Model
    79вҶ’
    80вҶ’### 4.1 Agent Profiles as Nix Expressions
    81вҶ’
    82вҶ’```nix
    83вҶ’# forge-profiles/profiles/base.nix
    84вҶ’{ forgeLib, ... }:
    85вҶ’forgeLib.mkAgent {
    86вҶ’  name = "base";
    87вҶ’  tools = [ "git" "gh" "ripgrep" "fd" ];
    88вҶ’  mcp-servers = [];
    89вҶ’  secrets = [];
    90вҶ’  resources = { cpu = 1; memory = "2Gi"; token-budget = 50000; };
    91вҶ’  permissions = {
    92вҶ’    repo = "read-write";
    93вҶ’    network = [];
    94вҶ’    spawn = { max-children = 5; require-approval-after = 3; };
    95вҶ’  };
    96вҶ’}
    97вҶ’```
    98вҶ’
    99вҶ’```nix
   100вҶ’# forge-profiles/profiles/security-reviewer.nix
   101вҶ’{ forgeLib, profiles, ... }:
   102вҶ’forgeLib.mkAgent {
   103вҶ’  name = "security-reviewer";
   104вҶ’  tools = profiles.base.tools ++ [ "semgrep" "trivy" "bandit" ];
   105вҶ’  mcp-servers = [ "github" ];
   106вҶ’  secrets = [ "GITHUB_TOKEN" ];
   107вҶ’  resources = { cpu = 2; memory = "4Gi"; token-budget = 100000; };
   108вҶ’  permissions = {
   109вҶ’    repo = "read-only";
   110вҶ’    network = [ "api.github.com" "*.semgrep.dev" ];
   111вҶ’    spawn = { max-children = 0; };
   112вҶ’  };
   113вҶ’}
   114вҶ’```
   115вҶ’
   116вҶ’```nix
   117вҶ’# forge-profiles/profiles/implementer.nix
   118вҶ’{ forgeLib, profiles, ... }:
   119вҶ’forgeLib.mkAgent {
   120вҶ’  name = "implementer";
   121вҶ’  tools = profiles.base.tools ++ [ "cargo" "rustc" "nodejs" "npm" ];
   122вҶ’  mcp-servers = [ "filesystem" "github" ];
   123вҶ’  secrets = [ "GITHUB_TOKEN" "NPM_TOKEN" ];
   124вҶ’  resources = { cpu = 4; memory = "8Gi"; token-budget = 200000; };
   125вҶ’  permissions = {
   126вҶ’    repo = "read-write";
   127вҶ’    network = [ "registry.npmjs.org" "crates.io" ];
   128вҶ’    spawn = { max-children = 10; require-approval-after = 5; };
   129вҶ’  };
   130вҶ’}
   131вҶ’```
   132вҶ’
   133вҶ’### 4.2 mkAgent Produces a Structured Attrset
   134вҶ’
   135вҶ’```nix
   136вҶ’# forgeLib/mkAgent.nix
   137вҶ’{ name, tools, mcp-servers, secrets, resources, permissions }:
   138вҶ’{
   139вҶ’  env = pkgs.buildEnv {
   140вҶ’    name = "forge-agent-${name}";
   141вҶ’    paths = map (t: pkgs.${t}) tools;
   142вҶ’  };
   143вҶ’  manifest = {
   144вҶ’    inherit name secrets resources permissions mcp-servers;
   145вҶ’    toolPaths = map (t: "${pkgs.${t}}/bin") tools;
   146вҶ’  };
   147вҶ’}
   148вҶ’```
   149вҶ’
   150вҶ’### 4.3 Agent Lifecycle State Machine
   151вҶ’
   152вҶ’```
   153вҶ’SpawnRequest вҶ’ PolicyCheck вҶ’ [NeedsApproval?] вҶ’ Approved
   154вҶ’    вҶ’ Materializing (nix build) вҶ’ Spawning (bwrap/docker)
   155вҶ’    вҶ’ Running вҶ’ Completed | Failed | Killed
   156вҶ’                    вҶ“
   157вҶ’              Cleanup (reap process, flush logs, release resources, notify parent)
   158вҶ’```
   159вҶ’
   160вҶ’**Detailed steps:**
   161вҶ’
   162вҶ’1. **Spawn request** вҖ” Primary agent (or CLI) requests spawn with profile name + task prompt
   163вҶ’2. **Nix evaluation** вҖ” Daemon runs `nix eval .#profiles.<name>.manifest` вҶ’ returns capability manifest as JSON. Cached after first eval.
   164вҶ’3. **Approval gate** вҖ” Policy engine checks: total agent count vs. caps, secrets vs. allowlist, network vs. allowlist, profile trust level. Gates for parent approval if soft cap exceeded.
   165вҶ’4. **Materialization** вҖ” Daemon runs `nix build .#profiles.<name>.env` вҶ’ produces `/nix/store/...-forge-agent-<name>/`. Cached after first build (subsequent spawns instant).
   166вҶ’5. **Namespace creation** вҖ” Bubblewrap invocation with: read-only Nix store bind, read-write workspace bind, per-agent UDS directory bind, PID namespace, `--die-with-parent`, cgroup limits.
   167вҶ’6. **Service binding** вҖ” Daemon creates per-agent UDS endpoints for auth, cache, vault, MCP, bus, and spawn API.
   168вҶ’7. **Running** вҖ” Agent executes with PATH pointing to Nix tools only, env vars for socket paths, cgroup-enforced resource limits, network namespace with allowlisted egress.
   169вҶ’8. **Termination** вҖ” Daemon reaps process, flushes logs, releases cgroup, removes UDS endpoints, notifies parent via bus, decrements spawn counter.
   170вҶ’
   171вҶ’### 4.4 Spawn Approval Model
   172вҶ’
   173вҶ’```
   174вҶ’permissions.spawn = {
   175вҶ’  max-children = 10;          # hard cap, never exceeded
   176вҶ’  require-approval-after = 5; # soft cap, parent asked after this
   177вҶ’};
   178вҶ’```
   179вҶ’
   180вҶ’- Agents 1вҖ“5: auto-approved, parent notified
   181вҶ’- Agents 6вҖ“10: parent must explicitly approve each spawn
   182вҶ’- Agent 11+: denied outright
   183вҶ’
   184вҶ’### 4.5 AgentRuntime Trait
   185вҶ’
   186вҶ’```rust
   187вҶ’#[async_trait]
   188вҶ’trait AgentRuntime: Send + Sync {
   189вҶ’    async fn spawn(&self, manifest: AgentManifest, workspace: &Path) -> Result<AgentHandle>;
   190вҶ’    async fn kill(&self, handle: &AgentHandle) -> Result<()>;
   191вҶ’    async fn status(&self, handle: &AgentHandle) -> Result<AgentStatus>;
   192вҶ’}
   193вҶ’
   194вҶ’struct BwrapRuntime { /* Linux namespace impl */ }
   195вҶ’struct DockerRuntime { /* Docker/macOS impl */ }
   196вҶ’struct HostRuntime { /* No-sandbox fallback, current behavior */ }
   197вҶ’```
   198вҶ’
   199вҶ’### 4.6 macOS Runtime
   200вҶ’

200вҶ’
   201вҶ’macOS cannot use Linux namespaces or bubblewrap. Rather than treating it as a degraded fallback, the macOS runtime is a first-class target with its own characteristics:
   202вҶ’
   203вҶ’**Primary option: Docker (via colima or Docker Desktop)**
   204вҶ’- Agent spawning latency: 1-3 seconds (vs. <1s on Linux with bwrap)
   205вҶ’- UDS sockets bind-mounted into containers via Docker volumes
   206вҶ’- File system performance: Use `:cached` mount flag for workspace volumes; expect ~2x latency vs. native on write-heavy workloads
   207вҶ’- Resource monitoring via Docker API (`bollard`) instead of cgroups
   208вҶ’- Network isolation via Docker bridge network with iptables rules
   209вҶ’
   210вҶ’**Lightweight option: Process isolation only (no container)**
   211вҶ’- Uses `sandbox-exec` (Apple's Sandbox framework) for basic filesystem restrictions where available
   212вҶ’- No network namespace isolation вҖ” network allowlist enforced at daemon proxy level only
   213вҶ’- No PID namespace вҖ” agents can see host processes (reduced security)
   214вҶ’- Sub-second spawning, native filesystem performance
   215вҶ’- Suitable for development/trusted environments, not production security
   216вҶ’
   217вҶ’**Auto-detection:** Daemon detects platform on startup and selects runtime:
   218вҶ’1. Linux + bwrap available вҶ’ `BwrapRuntime`
   219вҶ’2. macOS + Docker available вҶ’ `DockerRuntime`
   220вҶ’3. macOS + no Docker вҶ’ `HostRuntime` with `sandbox-exec` where available
   221вҶ’4. Any platform + `--host-runtime` flag вҶ’ `HostRuntime` (no isolation)
   222вҶ’
   223вҶ’**Documented performance expectations:**
   224вҶ’
   225вҶ’| Capability | Linux (bwrap) | macOS (Docker) | macOS (host) |
   226вҶ’|-----------|---------------|----------------|--------------|
   227вҶ’| Spawn latency | <1s | 1-3s | <0.5s |
   228вҶ’| Filesystem I/O | Native | ~2x slower | Native |
   229вҶ’| Network isolation | Full (namespace) | Full (bridge) | Proxy-only |
   230вҶ’| PID isolation | Full | Full | None |
   231вҶ’| Resource limits | cgroup v2 | Docker limits | None |
   232вҶ’| Security level | High | High | Low |
   233вҶ’
   234вҶ’### 4.7 Host Mode (No-Nix Fallback)
   235вҶ’
   236вҶ’Nix is powerful but optional. Agents can run in two modes:
   237вҶ’
   238вҶ’**Host mode** (default when Nix is not installed):
   239вҶ’- Agents run with the host PATH and environment (current forge behavior)
   240вҶ’- No reproducibility guarantees вҖ” tools must be pre-installed
   241вҶ’- Manifest is a TOML file instead of a Nix expression:
   242вҶ’
   243вҶ’```toml
   244вҶ’# .forge/profiles/implementer.toml
   245вҶ’[agent]
   246вҶ’name = "implementer"
   247вҶ’tools = ["cargo", "rustc", "nodejs"]  # checked in PATH at spawn time
   248вҶ’
   249вҶ’[resources]
   250вҶ’cpu = 4
   251вҶ’memory = "8Gi"
   252вҶ’
   253вҶ’[secrets]
   254вҶ’allowed = ["GITHUB_TOKEN"]
   255вҶ’
   256вҶ’[permissions]
   257вҶ’repo = "read-write"
   258вҶ’network = ["registry.npmjs.org", "crates.io"]
   259вҶ’
   260вҶ’[spawn]
   261вҶ’max-children = 10
   262вҶ’require-approval-after = 5
   263вҶ’```
   264вҶ’
   265вҶ’- Policy engine, approval gates, service ACLs, and audit trail all work identically
   266вҶ’- The daemon logs a warning at startup: "Running in host mode вҖ” agent environments are not reproducible"
   267вҶ’
   268вҶ’**Profile mode** (when Nix is installed):
   269вҶ’- Full Nix evaluation and materialization as described in Sections 4.1-4.4
   270вҶ’- First `nix build` for a profile may take 1-5 minutes (downloading packages to Nix store)
   271вҶ’- Subsequent spawns for the same profile are instant (Nix store cache)
   272вҶ’- `forge runtime warm <profile>` command pre-builds profiles before a run
   273вҶ’- Daemon tracks Nix store size and reports via metrics
   274вҶ’
   275вҶ’**Mode detection:** Daemon checks for `nix` binary in PATH. If present and `forge-profiles/flake.nix` exists, uses profile mode. Otherwise, host mode. Override with `--nix-mode` or `--host-mode` flags.
   276вҶ’
   277вҶ’### 4.8 Nix Evaluation Error Handling
   278вҶ’
   279вҶ’When `nix eval` or `nix build` fails:
   280вҶ’
   281вҶ’```
   282вҶ’SpawnRequest вҶ’ PolicyCheck вҶ’ Approved вҶ’ Materializing
   283вҶ’    вҶ’ nix eval fails вҶ’ MaterializationFailed { error, profile, stderr }
   284вҶ’        вҶ’ Daemon logs structured error
   285вҶ’        вҶ’ Parent agent notified via bus: AgentFailed { error: "Nix evaluation failed: ..." }
   286вҶ’        вҶ’ Agent status set to Failed in state store
   287вҶ’    вҶ’ nix build fails вҶ’ MaterializationFailed { error, profile, stderr }
   288вҶ’        вҶ’ Same notification flow
   289вҶ’        вҶ’ Common causes surfaced: missing package, network timeout, flake lock stale
   290вҶ’```
   291вҶ’
   292вҶ’Updated state machine:
   293вҶ’```
   294вҶ’SpawnRequest вҶ’ PolicyCheck вҶ’ [NeedsApproval?] вҶ’ Approved
   295вҶ’    вҶ’ Materializing вҶ’ [Success?]
   296вҶ’        вҶ’ Yes вҶ’ Spawning вҶ’ Running вҶ’ Completed | Failed | Killed
   297вҶ’        вҶ’ No  вҶ’ MaterializationFailed вҶ’ Cleanup (notify parent, log error)
   298вҶ’```
   299вҶ’
   300вҶ’## 5. Shared Services Architecture
   301вҶ’
   302вҶ’All services managed by the daemon, exposed to agents via Unix domain sockets. Agents never access services directly.
   303вҶ’
   304вҶ’### 5.1 Auth Proxy
   305вҶ’
   306вҶ’```
   307вҶ’Agent вҶ’ /run/forge/agent-$ID/auth.sock вҶ’ Daemon Auth Service вҶ’ External APIs
   308вҶ’```
   309вҶ’
   310вҶ’- Each agent gets an identity token (JWT, short-lived, scoped to manifest permissions) at spawn
   311вҶ’- Outbound API calls go through the proxy вҖ” daemon checks allowlist, injects credentials
   312вҶ’- Audit log: every external call logged with agent ID, target, timestamp
   313вҶ’- Agents never see raw secret values in the preferred (proxy-injection) path
   314вҶ’
   315вҶ’### 5.2 Key Vault
   316вҶ’
   317вҶ’```
   318вҶ’Agent вҶ’ /run/forge/agent-$ID/vault.sock вҶ’ Daemon Vault Service вҶ’ Encrypted Store
   319вҶ’```
   320вҶ’
   321вҶ’- Secrets stored encrypted at rest in `$FORGE_STATE_DIR/vault.db` (SQLite + age/AEAD)
   322вҶ’- Agent can only access secrets listed in its Nix manifest (daemon-enforced)
   323вҶ’- API: `get_secret(name) вҶ’ value`
   324вҶ’- Secret rotation: daemon rotates and notifies agents via bus
   325вҶ’- Secrets never touch agent filesystem
   326вҶ’
   327вҶ’### 5.3 Cache Service
   328вҶ’
   329вҶ’```
   330вҶ’Agent вҶ’ /run/forge/agent-$ID/cache.sock вҶ’ Daemon Cache Service вҶ’ Shared Cache
   331вҶ’```
   332вҶ’
   333вҶ’Three tiers:
   334вҶ’
   335вҶ’| Tier | Scope | Use Case | Backend |
   336вҶ’|------|-------|----------|---------|
   337вҶ’| L1: Agent-local | Single agent | Scratch, intermediates | In-memory |
   338вҶ’| L2: Project-shared | All agents on same repo | Git objects, build artifacts, file contents | Disk, content-addressed |
   339вҶ’| L3: Global | All agents | LLM response cache, tool output dedup | SQLite, content-addressed |
   340вҶ’
   341вҶ’- API: `get(namespace, key) вҶ’ Option<bytes>`, `put(namespace, key, value, ttl)`
   342вҶ’- LLM response caching keyed on `(model, system_prompt_hash, user_prompt_hash)`
   343вҶ’- Eviction: LRU per tier, configurable max size
   344вҶ’
   345вҶ’### 5.4 MCP Router
   346вҶ’
   347вҶ’```
   348вҶ’Agent вҶ’ /run/forge/agent-$ID/mcp.sock вҶ’ Daemon MCP Router вҶ’ MCP Server Pool
   349вҶ’```
   350вҶ’
   351вҶ’- Daemon manages a pool of MCP server processes (filesystem, github, database, custom)
   352вҶ’- Each agent's manifest declares which MCP servers it can access
   353вҶ’- Router multiplexes: multiple agents share one MCP server instance
   354вҶ’- Tool discovery: `list_tools()` returns only tools from allowed servers
   355вҶ’- Hot-pluggable: new MCP servers registered at runtime via daemon API
   356вҶ’
   357вҶ’### 5.5 Tool Registry
   358вҶ’
   359вҶ’Unified tool discovery across Nix CLI tools and MCP tools:
   360вҶ’
   361вҶ’- Agent sees single catalog: `{ "tools": [ "git", "gh", "semgrep", "github.create_pr", "fs.read_file", ... ] }`
   362вҶ’- CLI tools: available in PATH via Nix
   363вҶ’- MCP tools: proxied through router
   364вҶ’- Extension point: `.forge/tools/` for project-specific custom tools
   365вҶ’
   366вҶ’### 5.6 Message Bus
   367вҶ’
   368вҶ’```
   369вҶ’Agent вҶҗвҶ’ /run/forge/agent-$ID/bus.sock вҶҗвҶ’ Daemon Bus вҶҗвҶ’ Other Agents
   370вҶ’```
   371вҶ’
   372вҶ’**Three communication primitives:**
   373вҶ’
   374вҶ’1. **Request/Reply** вҖ” Agent A asks Agent B, blocks until response (with timeout)
   375вҶ’2. **Fire-and-forget** вҖ” Async notification, no reply expected
   376вҶ’3. **Broadcast** вҖ” Pub/sub within namespace (parent + all descendants)
   377вҶ’
   378вҶ’**Message types:**
   379вҶ’
   380вҶ’```rust
   381вҶ’enum BusMessage {
   382вҶ’    // Agent-to-agent
   383вҶ’    Request { from: AgentId, to: AgentId, payload: Value, reply_to: ChannelId },
   384вҶ’    Response { from: AgentId, to: ChannelId, payload: Value },
   385вҶ’    Broadcast { from: AgentId, topic: String, payload: Value },
   386вҶ’
   387вҶ’    // Spawn lifecycle
   388вҶ’    SpawnRequest { profile: String, overrides: AgentOverrides },
   389вҶ’    SpawnApprovalNeeded { child_manifest: AgentManifest, pending_id: SpawnId },
   390вҶ’    SpawnApproved { pending_id: SpawnId },
   391вҶ’    SpawnDenied { pending_id: SpawnId, reason: String },
   392вҶ’
   393вҶ’    // Daemon notifications
   394вҶ’    AgentSpawned { id: AgentId, name: String, parent: AgentId },
   395вҶ’    AgentCompleted { id: AgentId, result: AgentResult },
   396вҶ’    AgentFailed { id: AgentId, error: String },
   397вҶ’    SecretRotated { name: String },
   398вҶ’    Shutdown { reason: String, grace_period_ms: u64 },
   399вҶ’}

400вҶ’```
   401вҶ’
   402вҶ’**Routing rules:**
   403вҶ’- Parent вҶ” Child: always allowed
   404вҶ’- Sibling вҶ” Sibling: allowed within same parent's namespace
   405вҶ’- Cross-tree: denied by default, can be enabled in manifest
   406вҶ’- Broadcast: scoped to namespace
   407вҶ’
   408вҶ’## 6. Daemon Internals
   409вҶ’
   410вҶ’### 6.1 Internal Subsystems
   411вҶ’
   412вҶ’```
   413вҶ’forge-runtime process
   414вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ gRPC Server          (CLI + agent connections)
   415вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Agent Manager        (lifecycle, spawn tree, approval gates)
   416вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Nix Evaluator        (shell-out to nix, result caching)
   417вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Runtime Backend      (BwrapRuntime | DockerRuntime)
   418вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Service Manager      (starts/stops/monitors shared services)
   419вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Message Bus          (in-process pub/sub with UDS bridging)
   420вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Telemetry Collector  (logs, metrics, traces, audit)
   421вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ State Store          (SQLite for durable state)
   422вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ Policy Engine        (manifest validation against policies)
   423вҶ’```
   424вҶ’
   425вҶ’### 6.2 Agent Manager
   426вҶ’
   427вҶ’Maintains the agent tree in memory:
   428вҶ’
   429вҶ’```rust
   430вҶ’struct AgentTree {
   431вҶ’    agents: HashMap<AgentId, AgentNode>,
   432вҶ’    root: Option<AgentId>,
   433вҶ’}
   434вҶ’
   435вҶ’struct AgentNode {
   436вҶ’    id: AgentId,
   437вҶ’    parent: Option<AgentId>,
   438вҶ’    children: Vec<AgentId>,
   439вҶ’    manifest: AgentManifest,
   440вҶ’    handle: AgentHandle,
   441вҶ’    status: AgentStatus,
   442вҶ’    spawned_at: Instant,
   443вҶ’    spawn_count: u32,
   444вҶ’    resource_usage: ResourceSnapshot,
   445вҶ’}
   446вҶ’
   447вҶ’enum AgentStatus {
   448вҶ’    Pending,
   449вҶ’    Materializing,
   450вҶ’    Running { pid: u32, since: Instant },
   451вҶ’    Completed { result: AgentResult, duration: Duration },
   452вҶ’    Failed { error: String, duration: Duration },
   453вҶ’    Killed { reason: String },
   454вҶ’}
   455вҶ’```
   456вҶ’
   457вҶ’### 6.3 Nix Evaluator
   458вҶ’
   459вҶ’```rust
   460вҶ’struct NixEvaluator {
   461вҶ’    flake_path: PathBuf,
   462вҶ’    manifest_cache: DashMap<String, AgentManifest>,
   463вҶ’    build_cache: DashMap<String, PathBuf>,
   464вҶ’    eval_lock: tokio::sync::Semaphore,
   465вҶ’}
   466вҶ’```
   467вҶ’
   468вҶ’- `eval_manifest(profile)` вҖ” Fast, pure data evaluation via `nix eval`, cached
   469вҶ’- `build_env(profile)` вҖ” Slower first time, builds Nix derivation, cached in Nix store
   470вҶ’- Cache invalidation: flake lock file change clears caches
   471вҶ’
   472вҶ’### 6.4 Policy Engine
   473вҶ’
   474вҶ’Layered policy evaluation:
   475вҶ’
   476вҶ’```
   477вҶ’Global policy:    $FORGE_STATE_DIR/policy.toml
   478вҶ’Project policy:   .forge/policy.toml
   479вҶ’Agent manifest:   (from Nix eval)
   480вҶ’```
   481вҶ’
   482вҶ’```toml
   483вҶ’# .forge/policy.toml
   484вҶ’[limits]
   485вҶ’max_agents_total = 50
   486вҶ’max_agents_per_parent = 10
   487вҶ’max_depth = 4
   488вҶ’max_concurrent = 8
   489вҶ’
   490вҶ’[secrets]
   491вҶ’allowed = ["GITHUB_TOKEN", "NPM_TOKEN"]
   492вҶ’denied = ["AWS_ROOT_*"]
   493вҶ’
   494вҶ’[network]
   495вҶ’default = "deny"
   496вҶ’allowlist = ["api.github.com", "registry.npmjs.org", "crates.io"]
   497вҶ’
   498вҶ’[approval]
   499вҶ’auto_approve_profiles = ["base", "security-reviewer"]
   500вҶ’always_require_approval = ["implementer"]
   501вҶ’require_approval_after = 5
   502вҶ’```
   503вҶ’
   504вҶ’```toml
   505вҶ’[costs]
   506вҶ’max_tokens_per_agent = 200000     # hard cap per agent
   507вҶ’max_tokens_per_run = 2000000      # hard cap per run
   508вҶ’warn_at_percent = 80              # alert parent when agent hits 80% of budget
   509вҶ’```
   510вҶ’
   511вҶ’**Token budget enforcement:**
   512вҶ’- Daemon intercepts Claude CLI output and parses token usage from stream-json events
   513вҶ’- Cumulative token count tracked per agent and per run in state store
   514вҶ’- When agent hits `warn_at_percent` of its `token-budget`: daemon alerts parent via bus
   515вҶ’- When agent hits 100% of `token-budget`: daemon sends SIGTERM, status вҶ’ `Failed(token_budget_exceeded)`
   516вҶ’- When run hits `max_tokens_per_run`: daemon pauses all spawns, notifies CLI, requires manual approval to continue
   517вҶ’- Sub-agent tokens count toward both the sub-agent's budget AND the parent's cumulative tree budget
   518вҶ’- Cost attribution: `runs` table gains `total_tokens` and `estimated_cost_usd` columns
   519вҶ’
   520вҶ’**Evaluation order:**
   521вҶ’1. Global hard limits (max_agents_total, max_depth)
   522вҶ’2. Project limits (max_agents_per_parent, max_concurrent)
   523вҶ’3. Manifest secrets vs. policy allowlist/denylist
   524вҶ’4. Manifest network egress vs. policy allowlist
   525вҶ’5. Profile auto-approve or always-require-approval
   526вҶ’6. Soft cap check вҶ’ parent approval if exceeded
   527вҶ’
   528вҶ’### 6.5 State Store
   529вҶ’
   530вҶ’SQLite at `$FORGE_STATE_DIR/runtime.db`:
   531вҶ’
   532вҶ’```sql
   533вҶ’CREATE TABLE agents (
   534вҶ’    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   535вҶ’    parent_id   TEXT REFERENCES agents(id),
   536вҶ’    profile     TEXT NOT NULL,
   537вҶ’    manifest    TEXT NOT NULL,
   538вҶ’    status      TEXT NOT NULL,
   539вҶ’    pid         INTEGER,
   540вҶ’    started_at  TEXT,
   541вҶ’    finished_at TEXT,
   542вҶ’    result      TEXT,
   543вҶ’    resource_peak TEXT
   544вҶ’);
   545вҶ’
   546вҶ’CREATE TABLE audit_log (
   547вҶ’    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   548вҶ’    timestamp   TEXT NOT NULL,
   549вҶ’    agent_id    TEXT,
   550вҶ’    event_type  TEXT NOT NULL,
   551вҶ’    details     TEXT NOT NULL
   552вҶ’);
   553вҶ’
   554вҶ’CREATE TABLE secret_access (
   555вҶ’    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   556вҶ’    timestamp   TEXT NOT NULL,
   557вҶ’    agent_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
   558вҶ’    secret_name TEXT NOT NULL,
   559вҶ’    action      TEXT NOT NULL,
   560вҶ’    context     TEXT
   561вҶ’);
   562вҶ’
   563вҶ’CREATE TABLE runs (
   564вҶ’    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   565вҶ’    project     TEXT NOT NULL,
   566вҶ’    started_at  TEXT NOT NULL,
   567вҶ’    finished_at TEXT,
   568вҶ’    root_agent  TEXT REFERENCES agents(id),
   569вҶ’    policy      TEXT NOT NULL,
   570вҶ’    status      TEXT NOT NULL
   571вҶ’);
   572вҶ’```
   573вҶ’
   574вҶ’**Relationship to existing state:** The existing `StateManager` tracks phase/iteration progress (orchestration state). The daemon's state store tracks agent lifecycle, service access, and audit trail (runtime state). They complement each other.
   575вҶ’
   576вҶ’### 6.6 Orphan Recovery
   577вҶ’
   578вҶ’On startup, daemon:
   579вҶ’1. Scans for stale cgroup directories вҶ’ reap or adopt
   580вҶ’2. Cleans leftover UDS files in `/run/forge/`
   581вҶ’3. Marks incomplete state entries as `Failed(daemon_restart)`
   582вҶ’4. Adopts or kills running Docker containers with `forge-agent-` prefix
   583вҶ’
   584вҶ’### 6.7 Daemon Startup & Shutdown
   585вҶ’
   586вҶ’**Startup:** Load policy вҶ’ open/migrate DB вҶ’ start gRPC server вҶ’ start shared services вҶ’ recover orphans вҶ’ begin telemetry вҶ’ ready signal.
   587вҶ’
   588вҶ’**Shutdown (graceful):** Stop new spawns вҶ’ send Shutdown to all agents вҶ’ wait grace period вҶ’ force-kill remaining вҶ’ flush telemetry/audit вҶ’ close DB вҶ’ remove socket.
   589вҶ’
   590вҶ’### 6.8 Daemon Reliability
   591вҶ’
   592вҶ’The daemon is a single point of failure. Mitigations:
   593вҶ’
   594вҶ’**Process supervision:** The daemon should be managed by a process supervisor that auto-restarts on crash:
   595вҶ’- Linux: systemd unit file (`forge-runtime.service`) with `Restart=on-failure`, `RestartSec=2`
   596вҶ’- macOS: launchd plist (`com.forge.runtime.plist`) with `KeepAlive=true`
   597вҶ’- `forge runtime install` generates and installs the appropriate service file
   598вҶ’
   599вҶ’**Agent survival across daemon restarts:**

600вҶ’- `--die-with-parent` is used for bubblewrap agents (they die with daemon). This is intentional вҖ” agents without daemon services are non-functional (no vault, no bus, no MCP).
   601вҶ’- Docker agents survive daemon crashes (containers are independent). On restart, daemon re-adopts them via Docker API, reconnects UDS sockets, and resumes service proxying.
   602вҶ’- The state store (SQLite) persists across restarts. On recovery, daemon marks bwrap agents as `Failed(daemon_restart)` and re-adopts Docker agents.
   603вҶ’
   604вҶ’**Minimizing blast radius:**
   605вҶ’- Long-running agents should checkpoint their work to git (commit to worktree branch) periodically. The daemon can enforce this via a configurable `checkpoint_interval` that sends a checkpoint signal to agents.
   606вҶ’- The orchestration layer (CLI-side DAG scheduler) tracks completed phases. On daemon restart, only the in-progress phase needs re-execution вҖ” completed phases are not re-run.
   607вҶ’- Expected recovery time: daemon restart <5s, agent re-execution for the failed phase only.
   608вҶ’
   609вҶ’**Monitoring:**
   610вҶ’- Daemon exposes `/health` endpoint on a separate HTTP port for external monitoring (Prometheus, uptime checks)
   611вҶ’- `forge runtime status` command shows daemon uptime, agent count, resource usage
   612вҶ’- Daemon logs its own health metrics (goroutine count, memory usage, GC pressure)
   613вҶ’
   614вҶ’## 7. CLI вҶ” Daemon Integration
   615вҶ’
   616вҶ’### 7.1 What Stays in the CLI
   617вҶ’
   618вҶ’- All CLI commands (init, interview, generate, run, swarm, factory)
   619вҶ’- DAG scheduler (computes execution order)
   620вҶ’- Phase definitions and dependency resolution
   621вҶ’- Review specialist dispatch (reviewers become agents)
   622вҶ’- Factory API server, WebSocket, database
   623вҶ’- Configuration loading (forge.toml)
   624вҶ’- Orchestration state persistence (phase/iteration tracking)
   625вҶ’
   626вҶ’### 7.2 What Moves to forge-runtime
   627вҶ’
   628вҶ’- Process spawning (Command::new("claude") calls)
   629вҶ’- Process monitoring (PID tracking, stdout parsing)
   630вҶ’- Environment setup
   631вҶ’- Sandbox module (replaced by namespace isolation)
   632вҶ’
   633вҶ’### 7.3 gRPC Interface
   634вҶ’
   635вҶ’```protobuf
   636вҶ’syntax = "proto3";
   637вҶ’package forge.runtime.v1;
   638вҶ’
   639вҶ’service ForgeRuntime {
   640вҶ’    // Agent lifecycle
   641вҶ’    rpc SpawnAgent(SpawnRequest) returns (SpawnResponse);
   642вҶ’    rpc KillAgent(KillRequest) returns (KillResponse);
   643вҶ’    rpc GetAgent(GetAgentRequest) returns (AgentInfo);
   644вҶ’    rpc ListAgents(ListAgentsRequest) returns (ListAgentsResponse);
   645вҶ’
   646вҶ’    // Streaming output (replaces stdout pipe reading)
   647вҶ’    rpc StreamAgent(StreamAgentRequest) returns (stream AgentEvent);
   648вҶ’
   649вҶ’    // Run-level operations
   650вҶ’    rpc StartRun(StartRunRequest) returns (RunInfo);
   651вҶ’    rpc StopRun(StopRunRequest) returns (RunInfo);
   652вҶ’    rpc GetRun(GetRunRequest) returns (RunInfo);
   653вҶ’
   654вҶ’    // Approval flow
   655вҶ’    rpc PendingApprovals(PendingApprovalsRequest) returns (stream ApprovalRequest);
   656вҶ’    rpc ResolveApproval(ResolveApprovalRequest) returns (ResolveApprovalResponse);
   657вҶ’
   658вҶ’    // Service management
   659вҶ’    rpc RegisterMcpServer(McpServerConfig) returns (McpServerInfo);
   660вҶ’    rpc ListMcpServers(Empty) returns (McpServerList);
   661вҶ’
   662вҶ’    // Observability
   663вҶ’    rpc StreamEvents(StreamEventsRequest) returns (stream RuntimeEvent);
   664вҶ’    rpc GetMetrics(Empty) returns (MetricsSnapshot);
   665вҶ’
   666вҶ’    // Daemon management
   667вҶ’    rpc Health(Empty) returns (HealthResponse);
   668вҶ’    rpc Shutdown(ShutdownRequest) returns (Empty);
   669вҶ’}
   670вҶ’
   671вҶ’message SpawnRequest {
   672вҶ’    string run_id = 1;
   673вҶ’    string profile = 2;
   674вҶ’    string prompt = 3;
   675вҶ’    optional string parent_id = 4;
   676вҶ’    string workspace = 5;
   677вҶ’    map<string, string> overrides = 6;
   678вҶ’}
   679вҶ’
   680вҶ’message AgentEvent {
   681вҶ’    string agent_id = 1;
   682вҶ’    oneof event {
   683вҶ’        string stdout_line = 2;
   684вҶ’        Signal signal = 3;
   685вҶ’        string promise = 4;
   686вҶ’        SpawnChildRequest child_spawn = 5;
   687вҶ’        AgentExited exited = 6;
   688вҶ’    }
   689вҶ’}
   690вҶ’```
   691вҶ’
   692вҶ’### 7.4 Version Negotiation
   693вҶ’
   694вҶ’CLI and daemon are separate binaries that may be updated independently. Protocol:
   695вҶ’
   696вҶ’1. `HealthResponse` includes `protocol_version: u32` (starts at 1) and `daemon_version: String` (semver)
   697вҶ’2. CLI checks version on connect. If `protocol_version` matches, proceed normally
   698вҶ’3. If CLI protocol > daemon protocol: CLI warns "daemon is outdated, some features may not work" and falls back to the daemon's protocol version
   699вҶ’4. If daemon protocol > CLI protocol: daemon accepts the connection (backwards compatible) вҖ” new fields are ignored by the older CLI
   700вҶ’5. Protobuf schema follows standard forwards-compatible conventions: no field renumbering, no removing required fields, new fields are always optional
   701вҶ’
   702вҶ’**Breaking changes** (protocol version bump) require both binaries to be updated. The daemon refuses connections from CLIs with incompatible protocol versions and prints upgrade instructions.
   703вҶ’
   704вҶ’### 7.5 CLI вҶ” Daemon Interaction Patterns
   705вҶ’
   706вҶ’**Connection model:** CLI opens a persistent gRPC connection to the daemon for the duration of a run. Multiple CLI instances can connect simultaneously (e.g., two terminals running different projects).
   707вҶ’
   708вҶ’**CLI disconnect behavior:**
   709вҶ’- If CLI disconnects while agents are running, the daemon **continues execution** (agents are daemon children, not CLI children)
   710вҶ’- CLI can reconnect and resume streaming via `StreamAgent` / `StreamEvents` with a `since_timestamp` parameter
   711вҶ’- If no CLI reconnects within a configurable timeout (default: 1 hour), daemon completes the run and persists results to state store
   712вҶ’- `forge run --attach <run_id>` reconnects to an in-progress run
   713вҶ’
   714вҶ’**Sequence: Simple sequential run (3 phases):**
   715вҶ’```
   716вҶ’CLI                              Daemon                          Agents
   717вҶ’ в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   718вҶ’ в”ңв”Җ StartRun(phases=[1,2,3]) в”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   719вҶ’ в”Ӯв—„в”Җв”Җ RunInfo(run_id=R1) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   720вҶ’ в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   721вҶ’ в”ңв”Җ SpawnAgent(R1, phase1) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”ңв”Җ PolicyCheck в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ
   722вҶ’ в”ңв”Җ StreamAgent(agent-A) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ  nix build + bwrap spawn в”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ Agent A
   723вҶ’ в”Ӯв—„в”Җв”Җ AgentEvent(stdout) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ӯв—„в”Җв”Җ stdout lines в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ӯ
   724вҶ’ в”Ӯв—„в”Җв”Җ AgentEvent(promise) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ӯв—„в”Җв”Җ <promise>DONE</promise> в”Җв”Җв”Ӯ
   725вҶ’ в”Ӯ                                в”ңв”Җ Cleanup(agent-A) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ вң—
   726вҶ’ в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   727вҶ’ в”ңв”Җ SpawnAgent(R1, phase2) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”ңв”Җ spawn в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ Agent B
   728вҶ’ в”Ӯ  ... (same pattern) ...       в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   729вҶ’ в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   730вҶ’ в”ңв”Җ SpawnAgent(R1, phase3) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”ңв”Җ spawn в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ Agent C
   731вҶ’ в”Ӯ  ...                          в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   732вҶ’ в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   733вҶ’ в”ңв”Җ StopRun(R1) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   734вҶ’ в”Ӯв—„в”Җв”Җ RunInfo(status=complete) в”Җв”Җв”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   735вҶ’```
   736вҶ’
   737вҶ’**Sequence: DAG-parallel run (agents spawn sub-agents):**
   738вҶ’```
   739вҶ’CLI                              Daemon                          Agents
   740вҶ’ в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   741вҶ’ в”ңв”Җ StartRun(phases=DAG) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   742вҶ’ в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   743вҶ’ в”ңв”Җ SpawnAgent(phase1) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”ңв”Җ spawn в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ Agent A (primary)
   744вҶ’ в”ңв”Җ SpawnAgent(phase2) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”ңв”Җ spawn в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ Agent B
   745вҶ’ в”ңв”Җ StreamAgent(A) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   746вҶ’ в”ңв”Җ StreamAgent(B) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   747вҶ’ в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   748вҶ’ в”Ӯ  [Agent A requests sub-agent spawn via MCP tool]              в”Ӯ
   749вҶ’ в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯв—„в”Җв”Җ SpawnRequest в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ӯ Agent A
   750вҶ’ в”Ӯ                                в”ңв”Җ PolicyCheck (within cap)    в”Ӯ
   751вҶ’ в”Ӯ                                в”ңв”Җ spawn в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ Agent C (child of A)
   752вҶ’ в”Ӯв—„в”Җв”Җ AgentEvent(child_spawn) в”Җв”Җв”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   753вҶ’ в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   754вҶ’ в”Ӯ  [Agent A requests 6th child вҖ” exceeds soft cap]             в”Ӯ
   755вҶ’ в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯв—„в”Җв”Җ SpawnRequest в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ӯ Agent A
   756вҶ’ в”Ӯ                                в”ңв”Җ PolicyCheck (exceeds cap)   в”Ӯ
   757вҶ’ в”Ӯв—„в”Җв”Җ ApprovalRequest в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   758вҶ’ в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җ ResolveApproval(approve) в–әв”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
   759вҶ’ в”Ӯ                                в”ңв”Җ spawn в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ Agent D
   760вҶ’```
   761вҶ’
   762вҶ’**Multi-CLI:** Each CLI connection is independent. State is in the daemon. Multiple CLIs can:
   763вҶ’- Stream different agents' output simultaneously
   764вҶ’- One CLI runs `forge run`, another watches via `forge runtime events`
   765вҶ’- Factory UI connects as another client via WebSocket (daemon bridges gRPC вҶ’ WS)
   766вҶ’
   767вҶ’### 7.6 Integration Points
   768вҶ’
   769вҶ’**ClaudeRunner (runner.rs):** Replace `Command::new("claude")` with `runtime_client.spawn_agent()`. Stream output via `runtime_client.stream_agent()`. Same signal parsing logic, different source.
   770вҶ’
   771вҶ’**DagExecutor (executor.rs):** Unchanged вҖ” calls ClaudeRunner, which internally uses daemon.
   772вҶ’
   773вҶ’**SwarmExecutor (swarm/executor.rs):** Spawns a `swarm-coordinator` agent profile via daemon instead of direct CLI invocation.
   774вҶ’
   775вҶ’**Pipeline execution (factory/pipeline/execution.rs):** Uses `daemon.start_run()` instead of spawning forge subprocess.
   776вҶ’
   777вҶ’### 7.5 Crate Structure
   778вҶ’
   779вҶ’```
   780вҶ’forge/
   781вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Cargo.toml                    (workspace)
   782вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ crates/
   783вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ forge-cli/                (existing forge binary, refactored)
   784вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ src/
   785вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ main.rs
   786вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ cmd/
   787вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ orchestrator/

   789вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ swarm/
   790вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ review/
   791вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ factory/
   792вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ forge-runtime/            (NEW: daemon binary)

   796вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ nix_evaluator.rs

800вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ mod.rs        (AgentRuntime trait)
   801вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ bwrap.rs

   809вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ message_bus.rs

   817вҶ’в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ forge-common/             (NEW: shared types)
   818вҶ’в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ src/
   819вҶ’в”Ӯ           в”ңв”Җв”Җ manifest.rs
   820вҶ’в”Ӯ           в”ңв”Җв”Җ policy.rs
   821вҶ’в”Ӯ           в””в”Җв”Җ events.rs
   822вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ forge-profiles/               (NEW: Nix flake)
   823вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ flake.nix
   824вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ flake.lock
   825вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ lib/
   826вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ mkAgent.nix
   827вҶ’в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ profiles/
   828вҶ’в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ base.nix
   829вҶ’в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ implementer.nix
   830вҶ’в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ security-reviewer.nix
   831вҶ’в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ ...
   832вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ ui/                           (existing React frontend)
   833вҶ’```
   834вҶ’
   835вҶ’## 8. Inter-Agent Communication & Dynamic Teams
   836вҶ’
   837вҶ’### 8.1 Spawn MCP Tools
   838вҶ’
   839вҶ’Primary agents get spawn/coordination tools via their MCP socket:
   840вҶ’
   841вҶ’- `forge_spawn_agent(profile, task, wait)` вҖ” Spawn sub-agent
   842вҶ’- `forge_message_agent(agent_id, payload, wait_reply, timeout_secs)` вҖ” Message another agent
   843вҶ’- `forge_list_agents()` вҖ” List agents in namespace with status
   844вҶ’- `forge_kill_agent(agent_id, reason)` вҖ” Terminate a child
   845вҶ’- `forge_lock_files(paths, timeout_secs)` вҖ” Acquire workspace file locks
   846вҶ’- `forge_approve_spawn(pending_id)` вҖ” Approve a child's spawn request
   847вҶ’
   848вҶ’### 8.2 Workspace Coordination
   849вҶ’
   850вҶ’**Hybrid worktree model (default):**
   851вҶ’
   852вҶ’```
   853вҶ’/workspace/repo/                    (shared, read-only bind mount)
   854вҶ’/workspace/worktrees/
   855вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ agent-a/                        (git worktree, read-write)
   856вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ agent-b/                        (git worktree, read-write)
   857вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ agent-c/                        (git worktree, read-write)
   858вҶ’```
   859вҶ’
   860вҶ’- Agents read from shared repo (latest main)
   861вҶ’- Agents write to their own worktree
   862вҶ’- On completion, daemon merges worktree вҶ’ shared or creates PR branch
   863вҶ’- Merge conflicts вҶ’ parent agent notified for resolution
   864вҶ’
   865вҶ’**File locking protocol** (for shared workspace mode only вҖ” hybrid/worktree modes rarely need it):
   866вҶ’- Locks are **lease-based with TTL**: agent acquires lock with a TTL (default: 300s), must renew before expiry
   867вҶ’- Locks are **daemon-managed**: stored in-memory in the daemon, not on filesystem. No stale lockfiles.
   868вҶ’- **Crash safety**: if an agent dies while holding a lock, the daemon detects process termination and immediately releases all locks held by that agent
   869вҶ’- **Deadlock prevention**: locks are acquired in lexicographic path order. If agent A holds `src/a.rs` and requests `src/b.rs`, and agent B holds `src/b.rs` and requests `src/a.rs`, the daemon detects the cycle and rejects the second request with a `DeadlockDetected` error
   870вҶ’- **Granularity**: file-level only. Directory locks are not supported (lock individual files)
   871вҶ’- **When needed**: primarily in shared workspace mode where multiple agents write to the same checkout. In hybrid worktree mode, each agent has its own worktree, so file locks are only needed for shared config files (e.g., `Cargo.lock`, `package-lock.json`)
   872вҶ’
   873вҶ’**Alternative patterns:** Shared workspace + file locks (small teams), or full worktree isolation (independent modules). Configurable per-run.
   874вҶ’
   875вҶ’### 8.3 Agent Discovery
   876вҶ’
   877вҶ’Agents advertise capabilities on spawn, siblings discover by capability:
   878вҶ’
   879вҶ’```rust
   880вҶ’bus.advertise(capabilities: vec![
   881вҶ’    Capability::new("code-review", "Can review Rust and TypeScript"),
   882вҶ’]);
   883вҶ’
   884вҶ’let reviewers = bus.discover("code-review").await?;
   885вҶ’bus.request(to: reviewers[0].id, payload: review_request).await?;
   886вҶ’```
   887вҶ’
   888вҶ’## 9. Security Model
   889вҶ’
   890вҶ’### 9.1 Trust Boundaries
   891вҶ’
   892вҶ’| Layer | Trust Level | Description |
   893вҶ’|-------|------------|-------------|
   894вҶ’| `forge-runtime` daemon | Trusted | Manages all agents, holds vault master key, full host access |
   895вҶ’| `forge` CLI | Semi-trusted | Authenticated via local socket, cannot bypass policy |
   896вҶ’| Agent processes | Untrusted | Sandboxed, service access only through UDS with ACL |
   897вҶ’
   898вҶ’### 9.2 Four Isolation Layers
   899вҶ’
   900вҶ’**Filesystem:** Agents see only `/nix/store` (read-only), `/workspace` (bind mount), `/run/forge/agent-$ID` (own sockets), `/tmp` (private tmpfs). Cannot see other agents' sockets, daemon state, or host filesystem.
   901вҶ’
   902вҶ’**Process:** PID namespace (`--unshare-pid`), `--die-with-parent`, `--new-session`, cgroup v2 CPU+memory limits.
   903вҶ’
   904вҶ’**Network:** Private network namespace (`--unshare-net`), userspace networking via slirp4netns, daemon-side proxy enforces allowlisted hosts only. Auth injection at proxy level.
   905вҶ’
   906вҶ’**Service ACL:** Each UDS socket enforces per-agent ACL derived from manifest. Vault only returns allowed secrets, MCP router only exposes allowed servers, bus only routes within namespace.
   907вҶ’
   908вҶ’### 9.3 Secret Management
   909вҶ’
   910вҶ’1. Operator stores secrets via `forge vault set <name> <value>` вҶ’ encrypted with age вҶ’ stored in vault.db
   911вҶ’2. Policy binds secrets to allowed list per project
   912вҶ’3. Agent requests secret via vault.sock вҶ’ daemon checks manifest + policy вҶ’ grants or denies
   913вҶ’4. Preferred path: auth proxy injects credentials into outbound requests; agent never sees raw token
   914вҶ’5. Secrets never appear in env vars, agent filesystem, stdout capture, or shared cache
   915вҶ’
   916вҶ’### 9.4 Threat Mitigations
   917вҶ’
   918вҶ’| Threat | Mitigation |
   919вҶ’|--------|-----------|
   920вҶ’| Agent reads other agents' secrets | Per-agent UDS, vault ACL checks manifest |
   921вҶ’| Data exfiltration via network | Network namespace + allowlist proxy, all egress logged |
   922вҶ’| Sandbox escape | PID namespace + fs isolation + no root, bwrap suid-less |
   923вҶ’| Infinite sub-agent spawning | Hard cap in policy, parent approval gate |
   924вҶ’| Workspace corruption | Hybrid worktree model, agents write to own branch |
   925вҶ’| Compromised MCP server | Router validates responses, per-server resource limits |
   926вҶ’| Daemon crash orphans | `--die-with-parent`, startup recovery sweep |
   927вҶ’| Token leaked in stdout | Daemon-side regex scrubber on captured output |
   928вҶ’
   929вҶ’### 9.5 Audit Trail
   930вҶ’
   931вҶ’Append-only `audit_log` table. Events: AgentSpawned, AgentApproved, AgentDenied, SecretAccessed, NetworkCall, FileLockAcquired, FileModified, PolicyViolation, MessageSent, SpawnCapReached. Optionally forwarded to SIEM via OpenTelemetry.
   932вҶ’
   933вҶ’## 10. Observability & Telemetry
   934вҶ’
   935вҶ’### 10.1 Three Pillars
   936вҶ’
   937вҶ’**Structured logs:** Per-agent JSONL files + aggregated run log + optional OTLP export + WebSocket to Factory UI. Automatic logging of all service socket calls, network egress, resource samples, signal parsing вҖ” no agent cooperation needed.
   938вҶ’
   939вҶ’**Metrics:** Prometheus exposition format. Agent lifecycle counters, resource consumption gauges, service metrics (cache hit rates, MCP call durations, vault access counts), network egress tracking, run-level summaries.
   940вҶ’
   941вҶ’**Distributed traces:** Each run is a trace, each agent is a span, service calls are child spans. Trace context propagated from parent to child agent. Full execution tree viewable in Jaeger/Grafana Tempo.
   942вҶ’
   943вҶ’### 10.2 Factory UI Extensions
   944вҶ’
   945вҶ’New WebSocket message types: `AgentTreeUpdate` (live hierarchy), `ResourceSnapshot` (CPU/memory per agent), `ServiceEvent` (cache/vault/MCP/bus activity), `ApprovalRequired` (spawn approval queue).
   946вҶ’
   947вҶ’Dashboard views: agent tree visualization, resource heatmap, service timeline waterfall, message flow graph, approval queue with approve/deny, cost tracker (tokens per agent/run).
   948вҶ’
   949вҶ’### 10.3 Alerting
   950вҶ’
   951вҶ’Configurable thresholds in `$FORGE_STATE_DIR/alerts.toml`:
   952вҶ’- `agent_stuck`: running > 600s with no output > 120s вҶ’ notify parent
   953вҶ’- `memory_pressure`: > 90% of manifest memory вҶ’ warn parent
   954вҶ’- `spawn_storm`: > 10 spawns in 60s вҶ’ pause spawns
   955вҶ’- `policy_violations`: > 3 violations in 5m вҶ’ kill agent
   956вҶ’
   957вҶ’## 11. Data Retention & Cleanup
   958вҶ’
   959вҶ’Agent logs, audit entries, and state records grow unboundedly. Retention policies:
   960вҶ’
   961вҶ’**Configurable retention in `$FORGE_STATE_DIR/retention.toml`:**
   962вҶ’```toml
   963вҶ’[logs]
   964вҶ’max_runs = 100              # keep last N runs' log files
   965вҶ’max_age_days = 30           # delete logs older than this
   966вҶ’compress_after_days = 7     # gzip logs older than 7 days
   967вҶ’
   968вҶ’[audit]
   969вҶ’max_age_days = 90           # audit trail kept longer for compliance
   970вҶ’archive_format = "jsonl.gz" # compressed archive
   971вҶ’
   972вҶ’[state]
   973вҶ’max_runs = 500              # keep last N runs in runtime.db
   974вҶ’vacuum_on_cleanup = true    # SQLite VACUUM after deletion
   975вҶ’
   976вҶ’[nix]
   977вҶ’gc_after_days = 14          # nix store garbage collection for unused profiles
   978вҶ’```
   979вҶ’
   980вҶ’**Cleanup commands:**
   981вҶ’- `forge runtime gc` вҖ” manual cleanup: applies retention policies, runs Nix garbage collection
   982вҶ’- `forge runtime gc --dry-run` вҖ” shows what would be deleted
   983вҶ’- Daemon runs automatic cleanup daily (configurable interval) if running as a service
   984вҶ’
   985вҶ’## 12. MCP Router Validation
   986вҶ’
   987вҶ’The MCP router validates responses from MCP servers to mitigate compromised or misbehaving servers:
   988вҶ’
   989вҶ’- **Schema conformance**: responses must match the MCP protocol JSON-RPC schema. Malformed responses are dropped and logged as errors.
   990вҶ’- **Response size limits**: configurable max response size per server (default: 10MB). Oversized responses are truncated and the agent is notified.
   991вҶ’- **Timeout enforcement**: per-server timeout (default: 30s). Timed-out requests return an error to the agent; the router logs a warning and optionally restarts the server.
   992вҶ’- **Content scanning**: optional regex-based scanning for patterns that look like prompt injection attempts (e.g., `<system>`, `ignore previous instructions`). Suspicious responses are flagged in the audit log and optionally quarantined (agent gets a sanitized version).
   993вҶ’- **Rate limiting**: per-agent, per-server rate limits to prevent a single agent from overwhelming a shared MCP server (default: 100 calls/minute).
   994вҶ’
   995вҶ’## 13. Nix Profile Composition
   996вҶ’
   997вҶ’### 13.1 Forge Profiles vs. Project Profiles
   998вҶ’
   999вҶ’Forge ships a set of built-in profiles in `forge-profiles/`. Projects can extend or override them:
  1000вҶ’
  1001вҶ’```
  1002вҶ’forge-profiles/            (ships with forge вҖ” base, implementer, reviewer, etc.)
  1003вҶ’.forge/profiles/           (project-specific вҖ” overrides or new profiles)
  1004вҶ’```
  1005вҶ’
  1006вҶ’**Resolution order:**
  1007вҶ’1. Project profiles (`.forge/profiles/`) take precedence
  1008вҶ’2. Forge built-in profiles (`forge-profiles/`) as fallback
  1009вҶ’3. Project profile can import and extend a forge profile:
  1010вҶ’
  1011вҶ’```nix
  1012вҶ’# .forge/profiles/implementer.nix
  1013вҶ’{ forgeLib, forgeProfiles, ... }:
  1014вҶ’forgeProfiles.implementer.override {
  1015вҶ’  tools = forgeProfiles.implementer.tools ++ [ "python3" "poetry" ];
  1016вҶ’  secrets = forgeProfiles.implementer.secrets ++ [ "PYPI_TOKEN" ];
  1017вҶ’  permissions.network = forgeProfiles.implementer.permissions.network
  1018вҶ’    ++ [ "pypi.org" "files.pythonhosted.org" ];
  1019вҶ’}
  1020вҶ’```
  1021вҶ’
  1022вҶ’### 13.2 Interaction with Project Flakes
  1023вҶ’
  1024вҶ’If the target project has its own `flake.nix`:
  1025вҶ’- Forge profiles are a **separate flake** вҖ” they do not import or depend on the project flake
  1026вҶ’- The project's dev tools (from its flake) are available in the workspace, but agents use forge-profile-defined tools
  1027вҶ’- To make project-specific tools available to agents, add them to the project's `.forge/profiles/` override
  1028вҶ’- Tool version pinning: forge profiles use `forge-profiles/flake.lock`, project overrides use `.forge/profiles/flake.lock` (if it exists) or inherit from forge
  1029вҶ’
  1030вҶ’## 14. Migration Path
  1031вҶ’
  1032вҶ’### 14.1 From Current Architecture
  1033вҶ’
  1034вҶ’The refactor is **additive, not destructive**:
  1035вҶ’
  1036вҶ’1. Extract existing `src/` into `crates/forge-cli/`
  1037вҶ’2. Create `crates/forge-runtime/`, `crates/forge-proto/`, `crates/forge-common/`
  1038вҶ’3. Build `forge-runtime` daemon with agent manager + single runtime backend (bwrap or docker)
  1039вҶ’4. Replace `Command::new("claude")` in ClaudeRunner with `runtime_client.spawn_agent()`
  1040вҶ’5. Add services incrementally (message bus first, then cache, vault, MCP router, auth proxy)
  1041вҶ’6. Add Nix profiles for existing agent types (base, reviewer, implementer)
  1042вҶ’
  1043вҶ’### 14.2 Future: Docker/k8s Backend
  1044вҶ’
  1045вҶ’The `AgentRuntime` trait enables future backends:
  1046вҶ’- `DockerRuntime` already needed for macOS fallback
  1047вҶ’- `KubernetesRuntime` maps agent profiles to Pod specs
  1048вҶ’- Nix expressions can produce OCI images via `nix2container` for container backends
  1049вҶ’- Same gRPC interface, same policy engine, different execution substrate
  1050вҶ’

content.text:
Now I have the complete spec. Let me systematically review each previously identified issue.

---

## Spec Review: Second Pass

### Issue-by-Issue Assessment

**1. [Critical] No versioning or compatibility strategy for gRPC interface**

The spec now includes Section 7.4 "Version Negotiation" (lines 692-702). It defines:
- `protocol_version: u32` in `HealthResponse`
- Behavior for CLI protocol > daemon protocol (fallback with warning)
- Behavior for daemon protocol > CLI protocol (backwards compatibility)
- Protobuf forward-compatibility conventions
- Breaking change policy requiring both binaries to be updated

Additionally, the gRPC service is namespaced as `forge.runtime.v1` in the protobuf definition (line 638).

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**2. [Critical] macOS developer experience is second-class with Docker fallback**

Section 4.6 "macOS Runtime" (lines 200-233) now provides a dedicated treatment. It offers:
- Docker as the primary isolation option with documented perf characteristics
- A lightweight option using `sandbox-exec` without Docker
- Auto-detection logic for runtime selection
- A comparison table with spawn latency, filesystem I/O, network isolation, PID isolation, resource limits, and security level across all three backends

This is a substantial improvement -- macOS is treated as a first-class target with its own tradeoff matrix.

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**3. [Major] Nix as a hard dependency is a significant adoption barrier**

Section 4.7 "Host Mode (No-Nix Fallback)" (lines 234-275) addresses this. Key points:
- Nix is explicitly stated as optional (line 75: "Nix is optional")
- Host mode uses TOML profiles instead of Nix expressions
- Policy engine, approval gates, service ACLs, and audit trail all work identically
- Mode auto-detection with manual override flags

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**4. [Major] forge_lock_files mechanism is underspecified**

Section 8.2 "Workspace Coordination" (lines 865-871) now specifies:
- Lease-based locks with TTL (default 300s)
- Daemon-managed in-memory locks (no stale lockfiles)
- Crash safety: daemon detects process termination and releases locks
- Deadlock prevention: lexicographic path ordering with cycle detection
- File-level granularity only
- When locks are needed vs. not needed (shared workspace vs. hybrid worktree)

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**5. [Major] Daemon is a single point of failure with no HA story**

Section 6.8 "Daemon Reliability" (lines 592-612) now addresses this explicitly:
- Process supervision via systemd/launchd with auto-restart
- `forge runtime install` to generate service files
- Agent survival semantics (bwrap agents die intentionally, Docker agents are re-adopted)
- SQLite state persists across restarts
- Blast radius minimization via git checkpoints and orchestration-level phase tracking
- Expected recovery time: <5s for daemon restart
- Health endpoint for external monitoring

The spec honestly acknowledges the SPOF nature and provides practical mitigations rather than over-engineering HA for a single-node tool. This is the right call.

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**6. [Major] No resource accounting or cost control for LLM API calls**

Section 6.4 "Policy Engine" (lines 504-518) now includes a `[costs]` section with:
- `max_tokens_per_agent` and `max_tokens_per_run` hard caps
- `warn_at_percent` alert threshold
- Token usage parsing from Claude CLI stream-json events
- Budget enforcement: warning at threshold, SIGTERM at 100%, full-run pause at run cap
- Sub-agent tokens count toward parent's cumulative tree budget
- Cost attribution columns in the `runs` table

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**7. [Major] Relationship between existing orchestration and daemon is blurry**

Section 7.1-7.2 (lines 617-631) clearly delineates what stays in the CLI vs. what moves to the daemon. Section 6.5 (line 574) explicitly states: "The existing `StateManager` tracks phase/iteration progress (orchestration state). The daemon's state store tracks agent lifecycle, service access, and audit trail (runtime state). They complement each other."

Section 7.5 provides detailed sequence diagrams showing how CLI and daemon interact for sequential and DAG-parallel runs. Section 7.6 maps each existing component (ClaudeRunner, DagExecutor, SwarmExecutor, Pipeline) to its daemon integration point.

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**8. [Minor] $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is Linux-only**

Line 70 now includes a full socket path resolution chain: `$FORGE_SOCKET_PATH` (override) -> `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/forge.sock` (Linux) -> `$TMPDIR/forge-$UID/forge.sock` (macOS).

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**9. [Minor] No log/audit data retention or rotation**

Section 11 "Data Retention & Cleanup" (lines 957-984) adds:
- Configurable retention in `retention.toml`
- Separate policies for logs (max runs, max age, compression), audit (longer retention, compressed archives), state (max runs, vacuum), and Nix store GC
- `forge runtime gc` command with `--dry-run` option
- Automatic daily cleanup when running as a service

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**10. [Minor] MCP Router "validates responses" is vague**

Section 12 "MCP Router Validation" (lines 985-993) now specifies five concrete validation mechanisms:
- Schema conformance against MCP JSON-RPC schema
- Response size limits (default 10MB)
- Timeout enforcement (default 30s)
- Content scanning for prompt injection patterns
- Rate limiting (default 100 calls/min per agent per server)

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**11. [Minor] No discussion of Nix flake relationship to project flakes**

Section 13 "Nix Profile Composition" (lines 995-1029) addresses this:
- Section 13.1: forge built-in profiles vs. project profiles, with resolution order
- Section 13.2: explicit statement that forge profiles are a separate flake, do not import/depend on project flake
- Tool version pinning via separate flake.lock files
- Override mechanism to make project-specific tools available to agents

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**12. [Minor] Missing error handling for Nix evaluation failures**

Section 4.8 "Nix Evaluation Error Handling" (lines 277-298) adds:
- Explicit `MaterializationFailed` error state with error, profile, and stderr
- Structured error logging
- Parent notification via bus
- Agent status set to Failed in state store
- Common cause surfacing (missing package, network timeout, stale flake lock)
- Updated state machine diagram with the failure branch

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

### New Issues Introduced by Updates

**NEW-1. [Minor] Duplicate section numbering: Two sections labeled 7.5**

Section 7.4 is "Version Negotiation" (line 692), then 7.5 is "CLI <-> Daemon Interaction Patterns" (line 704), but the crate structure section at line 777 is also labeled "7.5 Crate Structure." This is a numbering error -- it should be 7.7 or the sections need renumbering.

**NEW-2. [Minor] `sandbox-exec` is deprecated on macOS**

Section 4.6 mentions using `sandbox-exec` (Apple's Sandbox framework) for lightweight macOS isolation (line 211). Apple has deprecated `sandbox-exec` and it may be removed in future macOS versions. The spec should note this deprecation risk and identify whether there is an alternative (e.g., App Sandbox entitlements, or simply documenting that the host-mode path on macOS has no filesystem isolation without Docker).

**NEW-3. [Minor] Daemon logs "goroutine count" but is written in Rust**

Line 612 says: "Daemon logs its own health metrics (goroutine count, memory usage, GC pressure)." The daemon is a Rust binary using tokio (line 71). Rust has neither goroutines nor a garbage collector. This should reference tokio task counts and allocator stats instead.

**NEW-4. [Minor] `/run/forge/` socket paths in service descriptions don't match the resolution logic**

The socket path resolution (line 70) specifies `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/forge.sock` or `$TMPDIR/forge-$UID/forge.sock`, but the service architecture diagrams (lines 307, 318, 330, 348, 369) all use hardcoded `/run/forge/agent-$ID/` paths. These should either use `$FORGE_RUNTIME_DIR` as a variable or note that `/run/forge/` is shorthand for the resolved runtime directory.

---

content.text:
Now I have the complete spec. Let me systematically review each previously identified issue.

---

## Spec Review: Second Pass

### Issue-by-Issue Assessment

**1. [Critical] No versioning or compatibility strategy for gRPC interface**

The spec now includes Section 7.4 "Version Negotiation" (lines 692-702). It defines:
- `protocol_version: u32` in `HealthResponse`
- Behavior for CLI protocol > daemon protocol (fallback with warning)
- Behavior for daemon protocol > CLI protocol (backwards compatibility)
- Protobuf forward-compatibility conventions
- Breaking change policy requiring both binaries to be updated

Additionally, the gRPC service is namespaced as `forge.runtime.v1` in the protobuf definition (line 638).

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**2. [Critical] macOS developer experience is second-class with Docker fallback**

Section 4.6 "macOS Runtime" (lines 200-233) now provides a dedicated treatment. It offers:
- Docker as the primary isolation option with documented perf characteristics
- A lightweight option using `sandbox-exec` without Docker
- Auto-detection logic for runtime selection
- A comparison table with spawn latency, filesystem I/O, network isolation, PID isolation, resource limits, and security level across all three backends

This is a substantial improvement -- macOS is treated as a first-class target with its own tradeoff matrix.

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**3. [Major] Nix as a hard dependency is a significant adoption barrier**

Section 4.7 "Host Mode (No-Nix Fallback)" (lines 234-275) addresses this. Key points:
- Nix is explicitly stated as optional (line 75: "Nix is optional")
- Host mode uses TOML profiles instead of Nix expressions
- Policy engine, approval gates, service ACLs, and audit trail all work identically
- Mode auto-detection with manual override flags

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**4. [Major] forge_lock_files mechanism is underspecified**

Section 8.2 "Workspace Coordination" (lines 865-871) now specifies:
- Lease-based locks with TTL (default 300s)
- Daemon-managed in-memory locks (no stale lockfiles)
- Crash safety: daemon detects process termination and releases locks
- Deadlock prevention: lexicographic path ordering with cycle detection
- File-level granularity only
- When locks are needed vs. not needed (shared workspace vs. hybrid worktree)

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**5. [Major] Daemon is a single point of failure with no HA story**

Section 6.8 "Daemon Reliability" (lines 592-612) now addresses this explicitly:
- Process supervision via systemd/launchd with auto-restart
- `forge runtime install` to generate service files
- Agent survival semantics (bwrap agents die intentionally, Docker agents are re-adopted)
- SQLite state persists across restarts
- Blast radius minimization via git checkpoints and orchestration-level phase tracking
- Expected recovery time: <5s for daemon restart
- Health endpoint for external monitoring

The spec honestly acknowledges the SPOF nature and provides practical mitigations rather than over-engineering HA for a single-node tool. This is the right call.

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**6. [Major] No resource accounting or cost control for LLM API calls**

Section 6.4 "Policy Engine" (lines 504-518) now includes a `[costs]` section with:
- `max_tokens_per_agent` and `max_tokens_per_run` hard caps
- `warn_at_percent` alert threshold
- Token usage parsing from Claude CLI stream-json events
- Budget enforcement: warning at threshold, SIGTERM at 100%, full-run pause at run cap
- Sub-agent tokens count toward parent's cumulative tree budget
- Cost attribution columns in the `runs` table

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**7. [Major] Relationship between existing orchestration and daemon is blurry**

Section 7.1-7.2 (lines 617-631) clearly delineates what stays in the CLI vs. what moves to the daemon. Section 6.5 (line 574) explicitly states: "The existing `StateManager` tracks phase/iteration progress (orchestration state). The daemon's state store tracks agent lifecycle, service access, and audit trail (runtime state). They complement each other."

Section 7.5 provides detailed sequence diagrams showing how CLI and daemon interact for sequential and DAG-parallel runs. Section 7.6 maps each existing component (ClaudeRunner, DagExecutor, SwarmExecutor, Pipeline) to its daemon integration point.

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**8. [Minor] $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is Linux-only**

Line 70 now includes a full socket path resolution chain: `$FORGE_SOCKET_PATH` (override) -> `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/forge.sock` (Linux) -> `$TMPDIR/forge-$UID/forge.sock` (macOS).

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**9. [Minor] No log/audit data retention or rotation**

Section 11 "Data Retention & Cleanup" (lines 957-984) adds:
- Configurable retention in `retention.toml`
- Separate policies for logs (max runs, max age, compression), audit (longer retention, compressed archives), state (max runs, vacuum), and Nix store GC
- `forge runtime gc` command with `--dry-run` option
- Automatic daily cleanup when running as a service

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**10. [Minor] MCP Router "validates responses" is vague**

Section 12 "MCP Router Validation" (lines 985-993) now specifies five concrete validation mechanisms:
- Schema conformance against MCP JSON-RPC schema
- Response size limits (default 10MB)
- Timeout enforcement (default 30s)
- Content scanning for prompt injection patterns
- Rate limiting (default 100 calls/min per agent per server)

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**11. [Minor] No discussion of Nix flake relationship to project flakes**

Section 13 "Nix Profile Composition" (lines 995-1029) addresses this:
- Section 13.1: forge built-in profiles vs. project profiles, with resolution order
- Section 13.2: explicit statement that forge profiles are a separate flake, do not import/depend on project flake
- Tool version pinning via separate flake.lock files
- Override mechanism to make project-specific tools available to agents

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

**12. [Minor] Missing error handling for Nix evaluation failures**

Section 4.8 "Nix Evaluation Error Handling" (lines 277-298) adds:
- Explicit `MaterializationFailed` error state with error, profile, and stderr
- Structured error logging
- Parent notification via bus
- Agent status set to Failed in state store
- Common cause surfacing (missing package, network timeout, stale flake lock)
- Updated state machine diagram with the failure branch

**Verdict: RESOLVED**

---

### New Issues Introduced by Updates

**NEW-1. [Minor] Duplicate section numbering: Two sections labeled 7.5**

Section 7.4 is "Version Negotiation" (line 692), then 7.5 is "CLI <-> Daemon Interaction Patterns" (line 704), but the crate structure section at line 777 is also labeled "7.5 Crate Structure." This is a numbering error -- it should be 7.7 or the sections need renumbering.

**NEW-2. [Minor] `sandbox-exec` is deprecated on macOS**

Section 4.6 mentions using `sandbox-exec` (Apple's Sandbox framework) for lightweight macOS isolation (line 211). Apple has deprecated `sandbox-exec` and it may be removed in future macOS versions. The spec should note this deprecation risk and identify whether there is an alternative (e.g., App Sandbox entitlements, or simply documenting that the host-mode path on macOS has no filesystem isolation without Docker).

**NEW-3. [Minor] Daemon logs "goroutine count" but is written in Rust**

Line 612 says: "Daemon logs its own health metrics (goroutine count, memory usage, GC pressure)." The daemon is a Rust binary using tokio (line 71). Rust has neither goroutines nor a garbage collector. This should reference tokio task counts and allocator stats instead.

**NEW-4. [Minor] `/run/forge/` socket paths in service descriptions don't match the resolution logic**

The socket path resolution (line 70) specifies `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/forge.sock` or `$TMPDIR/forge-$UID/forge.sock`, but the service architecture diagrams (lines 307, 318, 330, 348, 369) all use hardcoded `/run/forge/agent-$ID/` paths. These should either use `$FORGE_RUNTIME_DIR` as a variable or note that `/run/forge/` is shorthand for the resolved runtime directory.

---

## Summary

| # | Issue | Severity | Status |
|---|-------|----------|--------|
| 1 | gRPC versioning | Critical | RESOLVED |
| 2 | macOS experience | Critical | RESOLVED |
| 3 | Nix hard dependency | Major | RESOLVED |
| 4 | forge_lock_files underspecified | Major | RESOLVED |
| 5 | Daemon SPOF | Major | RESOLVED |
| 6 | LLM cost control | Major | RESOLVED |
| 7 | Orchestration/daemon relationship | Major | RESOLVED |
| 8 | XDG_RUNTIME_DIR Linux-only | Minor | RESOLVED |
| 9 | Log/audit retention | Minor | RESOLVED |
| 10 | MCP Router validation vague | Minor | RESOLVED |
| 11 | Nix flake relationship | Minor | RESOLVED |
| 12 | Nix error handling | Minor | RESOLVED |
| NEW-1 | Duplicate section 7.5 numbering | Minor | NEW |
| NEW-2 | sandbox-exec deprecated on macOS | Minor | NEW |
| NEW-3 | Go terminology in Rust daemon | Minor | NEW |
| NEW-4 | Hardcoded /run/forge/ paths vs. resolution logic | Minor | NEW |

All 12 original issues (2 Critical, 5 Major, 5 Minor) are **RESOLVED**.

4 new Minor issues were introduced.

---

**Final Verdict: APPROVED**

All critical and major issues have been thoroughly addressed. The 4 new issues are all minor (copy/terminology errors and a deprecation note) and do not affect the soundness of the design. The spec is ready to move forward, with the new minor items addressable as editorial fixes.
