1вҶ’//! Runtime event types for the Forge daemon event log and message bus.
     2вҶ’//!
     3вҶ’//! Every significant action in the runtime produces a `RuntimeEvent` that is
     4вҶ’//! persisted to the append-only event log. These events enable:
     5вҶ’//! - CLI attach/detach and replay without reconstructing state from stdout
     6вҶ’//! - Factory UI live updates via WebSocket
     7вҶ’//! - Audit trail for compliance
     8вҶ’//! - Daemon restart recovery via event replay
     9вҶ’
    10вҶ’use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
    11вҶ’use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
    12вҶ’use serde_json::Value;
    13вҶ’
    14вҶ’use crate::ids::{AgentId, ApprovalId, ChannelId, MilestoneId, RunId, SpawnId, TaskNodeId};
    15вҶ’use crate::manifest::{BudgetEnvelope, CapabilityEnvelope, MemoryScope};
    16вҶ’use crate::run_graph::{
    17вҶ’    AgentResult, ApprovalActorKind, PendingApproval, ResourceSnapshot, RunStatus, TaskStatus,
    18вҶ’};
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’/// A runtime event produced by the daemon and persisted to the event log.
    21вҶ’///
    22вҶ’/// Each event carries the run context (run ID, optional task/agent IDs),
    23вҶ’/// a timestamp, and a typed payload describing what happened.
    24вҶ’#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    25вҶ’pub struct RuntimeEvent {
    26вҶ’    /// Monotonically increasing sequence number within the event log.
    27вҶ’    pub seq: u64,
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’    /// The run this event belongs to.
    30вҶ’    pub run_id: RunId,
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’    /// The task node this event relates to, if applicable.
    33вҶ’    pub task_id: Option<TaskNodeId>,
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’    /// The agent instance this event relates to, if applicable.
    36вҶ’    pub agent_id: Option<AgentId>,
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’    /// When this event occurred.
    39вҶ’    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
    40вҶ’
    41вҶ’    /// The event payload.
    42вҶ’    pub event: RuntimeEventKind,
    43вҶ’}
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’/// All possible runtime event kinds, covering the full agent lifecycle,
    46вҶ’/// approval flow, service interactions, and daemon management.
    47вҶ’#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    48вҶ’pub enum RuntimeEventKind {
    49вҶ’    // в”Җв”Җ Run lifecycle в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
    50вҶ’
    51вҶ’    /// A new run was submitted to the daemon.
    52вҶ’    RunSubmitted {
    53вҶ’        /// Project path or name.
    54вҶ’        project: String,
    55вҶ’        /// Number of milestones in the submitted plan.
    56вҶ’        milestone_count: usize,
    57вҶ’    },
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’    /// The overall run status changed.
    60вҶ’    RunStatusChanged {
    61вҶ’        /// Previous status.
    62вҶ’        from: RunStatus,
    63вҶ’        /// New status.
    64вҶ’        to: RunStatus,
    65вҶ’    },
    66вҶ’
    67вҶ’    /// A milestone was completed (all its tasks finished).
    68вҶ’    MilestoneCompleted {
    69вҶ’        /// The completed milestone.
    70вҶ’        milestone_id: MilestoneId,
    71вҶ’    },
    72вҶ’
    73вҶ’    /// The run finished (terminal status reached).
    74вҶ’    RunFinished {
    75вҶ’        /// Final run status.
    76вҶ’        status: RunStatus,
    77вҶ’        /// Total tokens consumed across all tasks.
    78вҶ’        total_tokens: u64,
    79вҶ’        /// Estimated cost in USD.
    80вҶ’        estimated_cost_usd: f64,
    81вҶ’    },
    82вҶ’
    83вҶ’    // в”Җв”Җ Task lifecycle в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
    84вҶ’
    85вҶ’    /// A new task node was created in the run graph.
    86вҶ’    TaskCreated {
    87вҶ’        /// The parent task, if this is a dynamically spawned child.
    88вҶ’        parent_task_id: Option<TaskNodeId>,
    89вҶ’        /// The task's objective.
    90вҶ’        objective: String,
    91вҶ’        /// The profile used for this task.
    92вҶ’        profile: String,
    93вҶ’    },
    94вҶ’
    95вҶ’    /// A task's status changed.
    96вҶ’    TaskStatusChanged {
    97вҶ’        /// Previous status.
    98вҶ’        from: TaskStatus,
    99вҶ’        /// New status.

   368вҶ’/// Message types for the inter-agent message bus.
   369вҶ’///
   370вҶ’/// The daemon routes messages between task nodes based on namespace rules:
   371вҶ’/// parent-child always allowed, siblings within same parent allowed,
   372вҶ’/// cross-tree denied by default.
   373вҶ’#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   374вҶ’pub enum BusMessage {
   375вҶ’    // в”Җв”Җ Task-to-task communication в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
   376вҶ’
   377вҶ’    /// Request/reply message from one task to another (blocks until response).
   378вҶ’    Request {
   379вҶ’        /// Sending task.
   380вҶ’        from: TaskNodeId,
   381вҶ’        /// Target task.
   382вҶ’        to: TaskNodeId,
   383вҶ’        /// Message payload.
   384вҶ’        payload: Value,
   385вҶ’        /// Channel to send the reply to.
   386вҶ’        reply_to: ChannelId,
   387вҶ’    },
   388вҶ’
   389вҶ’    /// Response to a previous request.
   390вҶ’    Response {
   391вҶ’        /// Responding task.
   392вҶ’        from: TaskNodeId,
   393вҶ’        /// Channel the response is sent to.
   394вҶ’        to: ChannelId,
   395вҶ’        /// Response payload.
   396вҶ’        payload: Value,
   397вҶ’    },
   398вҶ’
   399вҶ’    /// Broadcast message to all tasks in a namespace (pub/sub).
   400вҶ’    Broadcast {
   401вҶ’        /// Sending task.
   402вҶ’        from: TaskNodeId,
   403вҶ’        /// Topic for routing.
   404вҶ’        topic: String,
   405вҶ’        /// Message payload.
   406вҶ’        payload: Value,
   407вҶ’    },
   408вҶ’
   409вҶ’    // в”Җв”Җ Child task lifecycle messages в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
   410вҶ’
   411вҶ’    /// A parent agent requests creation of a child task.
   412вҶ’    ChildTaskRequested {
   413вҶ’        /// Profile for the child agent.
   414вҶ’        profile: String,
   415вҶ’        /// Milestone the child must attach to.
   416вҶ’        milestone_id: MilestoneId,
   417вҶ’        /// Objective for the child task.
   418вҶ’        objective: String,
   419вҶ’        /// Expected output description for the child task.
   420вҶ’        expected_output: String,
   421вҶ’        /// Budget requested for the child task.
   422вҶ’        budget: BudgetEnvelope,
   423вҶ’        /// Memory scope for the child task.
   424вҶ’        memory_scope: MemoryScope,
   425вҶ’        /// Dependency edges in the run graph.
   426вҶ’        depends_on: Vec<TaskNodeId>,
   427вҶ’        /// Requested capability envelope.
   428вҶ’        requested_capabilities: CapabilityEnvelope,
   429вҶ’        /// Whether the parent waits for completion before continuing.
   430вҶ’        wait_for_completion: bool,
   431вҶ’        /// Spawn request ID.
   432вҶ’        pending_id: SpawnId,
   433вҶ’    },
   434вҶ’
   435вҶ’    /// A child task creation requires approval from the parent or operator.
   436вҶ’    ChildTaskApprovalNeeded {
   437вҶ’        /// Full pending approval generated by the daemon.
   438вҶ’        approval: PendingApproval,
   439вҶ’    },
   440вҶ’
   441вҶ’    /// A pending child task was approved.
   442вҶ’    ChildTaskApproved {
   443вҶ’        /// The approved spawn.
   444вҶ’        pending_id: SpawnId,
   445вҶ’    },
   446вҶ’
   447вҶ’    /// A pending child task was denied.
   448вҶ’    ChildTaskDenied {
   449вҶ’        /// The denied spawn.
   450вҶ’        pending_id: SpawnId,
   451вҶ’        /// Reason for denial.
   452вҶ’        reason: String,
   453вҶ’    },
   454вҶ’
   455вҶ’    // в”Җв”Җ Daemon notifications в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
   456вҶ’
   457вҶ’    /// An agent was spawned.
   458вҶ’    AgentSpawned {
   459вҶ’        /// The agent's ID.
   460вҶ’        id: AgentId,
   461вҶ’        /// The task this agent is executing.
   462вҶ’        task_id: TaskNodeId,
   463вҶ’        /// Parent task that owns this task.
   464вҶ’        parent_task: Option<TaskNodeId>,
   465вҶ’    },
   466вҶ’
   467вҶ’    /// A task completed successfully.
   468вҶ’    TaskCompleted {
   469вҶ’        /// The completed task.
   470вҶ’        id: TaskNodeId,
   471вҶ’        /// Result summary.
   472вҶ’        result: AgentResult,
   473вҶ’    },
   474вҶ’
   475вҶ’    /// A task failed.
   476вҶ’    TaskFailed {
   477вҶ’        /// The failed task.
   478вҶ’        id: TaskNodeId,
   479вҶ’        /// Error description.
   480вҶ’        error: String,
   481вҶ’    },
   482вҶ’
   483вҶ’    /// A memory entry was promoted to a higher scope.
   484вҶ’    MemoryPromoted {
   485вҶ’        /// The promoted entry.
   486вҶ’        entry_id: String,
   487вҶ’        /// Target scope name.
   488вҶ’        scope: String,
   489вҶ’    },
   490вҶ’
   491вҶ’    /// A secret was rotated; agents holding scoped credentials should refresh.
   492вҶ’    SecretRotated {
   493вҶ’        /// The rotated secret name.
   494вҶ’        name: String,
   495вҶ’    },
   496вҶ’
   497вҶ’    /// The daemon is shutting down; agents should checkpoint and exit.
   498вҶ’    Shutdown {
   499вҶ’        /// Reason for shutdown.
   500вҶ’        reason: String,
   501вҶ’        /// Grace period before force-kill, in milliseconds.
   502вҶ’        grace_period_ms: u64,
   503вҶ’    },
   504вҶ’}
   505вҶ’
   506вҶ’/// Event describing task output (stdout/stderr) streamed from an agent.
   507вҶ’///
   508вҶ’/// Used by the `StreamTaskOutput` gRPC endpoint to deliver live agent output
   509вҶ’/// to attached CLI clients.
   510вҶ’#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   511вҶ’pub struct TaskOutputEvent {
   512вҶ’    /// The run producing this output.
   513вҶ’    pub run_id: RunId,
   514вҶ’
   515вҶ’    /// The task producing this output.
   516вҶ’    pub task_id: TaskNodeId,
   517вҶ’
   518вҶ’    /// The agent producing this output.
   519вҶ’    pub agent_id: AgentId,
   520вҶ’
   521вҶ’    /// Authoritative runtime event-log cursor for this output chunk.
   522вҶ’    pub cursor: u64,
   523вҶ’
   524вҶ’    /// The output payload.
   525вҶ’    pub output: TaskOutput,
   526вҶ’
   527вҶ’    /// When this output was captured.
   528вҶ’    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
   529вҶ’}
   530вҶ’
   531вҶ’/// A chunk of task output from an agent.
   532вҶ’#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   533вҶ’pub enum TaskOutput {
   534вҶ’    /// Standard output line.
   535вҶ’    Stdout(String),
   536вҶ’
   537вҶ’    /// Standard error line.
   538вҶ’    Stderr(String),
   539вҶ’
   540вҶ’    /// A signal tag parsed from agent output (e.g., `<progress>`, `<blocker>`).
   541вҶ’    Signal {
   542вҶ’        /// Signal type name.
   543вҶ’        kind: String,
   544вҶ’        /// Signal payload.
   545вҶ’        content: String,
   546вҶ’    },
   547вҶ’
   548вҶ’    /// Token usage update parsed from the agent's stream-json output.
   549вҶ’    TokenUsage {
   550вҶ’        /// Tokens consumed in this update.
   551вҶ’        tokens: u64,
   552вҶ’        /// Cumulative tokens for this agent.
   553вҶ’        cumulative: u64,
   554вҶ’    },
   555вҶ’
   556вҶ’    /// The agent emitted `<promise>DONE</promise>`, signaling task completion.
   557вҶ’    PromiseDone,
   558вҶ’}
   559вҶ’
   560вҶ’#[cfg(test)]
   561вҶ’mod tests {
   562вҶ’    use super::*;
   563вҶ’
   564вҶ’    #[test]
   565вҶ’    fn runtime_event_serde_roundtrip() {
   566вҶ’        let event = RuntimeEvent {
   567вҶ’            seq: 1,
   568вҶ’            run_id: RunId::from("run-1"),
   569вҶ’            task_id: Some(TaskNodeId::from("task-1")),
   570вҶ’            agent_id: None,
   571вҶ’            timestamp: Utc::now(),
   572вҶ’            event: RuntimeEventKind::RunSubmitted {
   573вҶ’                project: "test-project".into(),
   574вҶ’                milestone_count: 3,
   575вҶ’            },
   576вҶ’        };
   577вҶ’
   578вҶ’        let json = serde_json::to_string(&event).unwrap();
   579вҶ’        let back: RuntimeEvent = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
   580вҶ’        assert_eq!(back.seq, 1);
   581вҶ’    }
   582вҶ’
   583вҶ’    #[test]
   584вҶ’    fn bus_message_serde_roundtrip() {
   585вҶ’        let msg = BusMessage::Broadcast {
   586вҶ’            from: TaskNodeId::from("task-1"),
   587вҶ’            topic: "status-update".into(),
   588вҶ’            payload: serde_json::json!({"progress": 0.5}),
   589вҶ’        };
   590вҶ’
   591вҶ’        let json = serde_json::to_string(&msg).unwrap();
   592вҶ’        let back: BusMessage = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
   593вҶ’        match back {
   594вҶ’            BusMessage::Broadcast { topic, .. } => assert_eq!(topic, "status-update"),
   595вҶ’            _ => panic!("wrong variant"),
   596вҶ’        }
   597вҶ’    }
   598вҶ’
   599вҶ’    #[test]
   600вҶ’    fn task_output_event_variants() {
   601вҶ’        let events = vec![
   602вҶ’            TaskOutput::Stdout("hello world".into()),
   603вҶ’            TaskOutput::Stderr("warning: unused variable".into()),
   604вҶ’            TaskOutput::Signal {
   605вҶ’                kind: "progress".into(),
   606вҶ’                content: "50%".into(),
   607вҶ’            },
   608вҶ’            TaskOutput::TokenUsage {
   609вҶ’                tokens: 150,
   610вҶ’                cumulative: 1500,
   611вҶ’            },
   612вҶ’            TaskOutput::PromiseDone,
   613вҶ’        ];
   614вҶ’
   615вҶ’        for event in events {
   616вҶ’            let json = serde_json::to_string(&event).unwrap();
   617вҶ’            let _back: TaskOutput = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
   618вҶ’        }
   619вҶ’    }
   620вҶ’}

1вҶ’//! Agent runtime trait and associated types.
     2вҶ’//!
     3вҶ’//! The `AgentRuntime` trait abstracts over the concrete execution backend
     4вҶ’//! (bubblewrap, Docker, or host process). The daemon selects the appropriate
     5вҶ’//! implementation at startup based on platform detection and operator configuration.
     6вҶ’//!
     7вҶ’//! Each backend is responsible for creating an isolated execution environment,
     8вҶ’//! binding service sockets, enforcing resource limits, and reaping the process
     9вҶ’//! on completion.
    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’use std::path::Path;
    12вҶ’
    13вҶ’use async_trait::async_trait;
    14вҶ’
    15вҶ’use crate::manifest::CompiledProfile;
    16вҶ’use crate::run_graph::{AgentHandle, TaskNode};
    17вҶ’
    18вҶ’/// Status of an agent instance as reported by the runtime backend.
    19вҶ’#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
    20вҶ’pub enum AgentStatus {
    21вҶ’    /// The agent is being set up (environment materializing, sockets binding).
    22вҶ’    Initializing,
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’    /// The agent process/container is running.
    25вҶ’    Running,
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’    /// The agent exited successfully (exit code 0).
    28вҶ’    Exited {
    29вҶ’        /// Process exit code.
    30вҶ’        exit_code: i32,
    31вҶ’    },
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’    /// The agent was killed by the daemon or operator.
    34вҶ’    Killed {
    35вҶ’        /// Signal or reason for the kill.
    36вҶ’        reason: String,
    37вҶ’    },
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’    /// The agent process/container crashed unexpectedly.
    40вҶ’    Crashed {
    41вҶ’        /// Exit code if available.
    42вҶ’        exit_code: Option<i32>,
    43вҶ’        /// Error message or signal description.
    44вҶ’        error: String,
    45вҶ’    },
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’    /// The agent's status is unknown (e.g., after daemon restart for bwrap agents).
    48вҶ’    Unknown,
    49вҶ’}
    50вҶ’
    51вҶ’/// Trait abstracting over agent execution backends.
    52вҶ’///
    53вҶ’/// Implementations:
    54вҶ’/// - `BwrapRuntime`: Linux namespace isolation via bubblewrap (primary on Linux)
    55вҶ’/// - `DockerRuntime`: Docker container isolation (primary on macOS, optional on Linux)
    56вҶ’/// - `HostRuntime`: No isolation, explicit insecure fallback for development
    57вҶ’///
    58вҶ’/// The daemon holds a `Box<dyn AgentRuntime>` selected at startup based on
    59вҶ’/// platform detection. All agent lifecycle operations go through this trait.
    60вҶ’#[async_trait]
    61вҶ’pub trait AgentRuntime: Send + Sync {
    62вҶ’    /// Spawn an agent to execute the given task node.
    63вҶ’    ///
    64вҶ’    /// The runtime backend must:
    65вҶ’    /// 1. Materialize the environment from the compiled profile's env plan
    66вҶ’    /// 2. Create the isolated execution context (namespace, container, or process)
    67вҶ’    /// 3. Bind the workspace at the given path
    68вҶ’    /// 4. Create per-agent UDS socket directory and bind service endpoints
    69вҶ’    /// 5. Set resource limits (CPU, memory) from the profile's manifest
    70вҶ’    /// 6. Start the agent process with `--die-with-parent` semantics (where supported)
    71вҶ’    /// 7. Return an `AgentHandle` for subsequent lifecycle operations
    72вҶ’    ///
    73вҶ’    /// # Arguments
    74вҶ’    /// * `profile` - The compiled profile defining capabilities and environment
    75вҶ’    /// * `task` - The task node this agent will execute
    76вҶ’    /// * `workspace` - Path to the agent's workspace directory (worktree or shared)
    77вҶ’    ///
    78вҶ’    /// # Errors
    79вҶ’    /// Returns an error if environment materialization, namespace creation,
    80вҶ’    /// or process spawning fails.
    81вҶ’    async fn spawn(
    82вҶ’        &self,
    83вҶ’        profile: CompiledProfile,
    84вҶ’        task: TaskNode,
    85вҶ’        workspace: &Path,
    86вҶ’    ) -> anyhow::Result<AgentHandle>;
    87вҶ’
    88вҶ’    /// Kill an agent process/container.
    89вҶ’    ///
    90вҶ’    /// Sends a termination signal (SIGTERM for processes, stop for containers)
    91вҶ’    /// and waits for the agent to exit. The caller should set a timeout and
    92вҶ’    /// escalate to force-kill if needed.
    93вҶ’    ///
    94вҶ’    /// # Errors
    95вҶ’    /// Returns an error if the kill signal cannot be delivered (e.g., process
    96вҶ’    /// already exited, container not found).
    97вҶ’    async fn kill(&self, handle: &AgentHandle) -> anyhow::Result<()>;
    98вҶ’
    99вҶ’    /// Query the current status of an agent.
   100вҶ’    ///
   101вҶ’    /// For bwrap/host agents, this checks the process status via PID.
   102вҶ’    /// For Docker agents, this queries the container state via Docker API.
   103вҶ’    ///
   104вҶ’    /// # Errors
   105вҶ’    /// Returns an error if the status cannot be determined (e.g., PID not found
   106вҶ’    /// and no container ID available).
   107вҶ’    async fn status(&self, handle: &AgentHandle) -> anyhow::Result<AgentStatus>;
   108вҶ’}
   109вҶ’
   110вҶ’#[cfg(test)]
   111вҶ’mod tests {
   112вҶ’    use super::*;
   113вҶ’
   114вҶ’    #[test]
   115вҶ’    fn agent_status_serde_roundtrip() {
   116вҶ’        let statuses = vec![
   117вҶ’            AgentStatus::Initializing,
   118вҶ’            AgentStatus::Running,
   119вҶ’            AgentStatus::Exited { exit_code: 0 },
   120вҶ’            AgentStatus::Killed {
   121вҶ’                reason: "budget exceeded".into(),
   122вҶ’            },
   123вҶ’            AgentStatus::Crashed {
   124вҶ’                exit_code: Some(137),
   125вҶ’                error: "OOM killed".into(),
   126вҶ’            },
   127вҶ’            AgentStatus::Unknown,
   128вҶ’        ];
   129вҶ’
   130вҶ’        for status in statuses {
   131вҶ’            let json = serde_json::to_string(&status).unwrap();
   132вҶ’            let back: AgentStatus = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
   133вҶ’            assert_eq!(status, back);
   134вҶ’        }
   135вҶ’    }
   136вҶ’}
   137вҶ’

1вҶ’[package]

1вҶ’# Spawn Site Migration Checklist
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’**Date:** 2026-03-13
     4вҶ’**Purpose:** Comprehensive audit of every process-spawning site in `src/` that must be migrated to the `forge-runtime` daemon (per the [Runtime Platform Design](./2026-03-13-forge-runtime-platform-design.md), Sections 7.6 and 14).
     5вҶ’
     6вҶ’**Legend:**
     7вҶ’- **Daemon API** = the gRPC call or facade method that replaces the spawn
     8вҶ’- **Complexity** = Low / Medium / High based on stdout parsing, lifecycle management, and coordination logic that must move
     9вҶ’
    10вҶ’---
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’## Part A: Claude CLI Spawn Sites (Agent Execution)
    13вҶ’
    14вҶ’These are the high-value migration targets. Each spawns a Claude CLI process, pipes a prompt to stdin, and parses structured output from stdout.
    15вҶ’
    16вҶ’---
    17вҶ’
    18вҶ’### A1. Orchestrator Runner вҖ” Sequential Phase Execution
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’| Field | Value |
    21вҶ’|-------|-------|
    22вҶ’| **File** | `src/orchestrator/runner.rs` |
    23вҶ’| **Line** | 511 (Command::new), 559 (spawn) |
    24вҶ’| **Subsystem** | Orchestrator (sequential `forge run`) |
    25вҶ’| **Current pattern** | Spawns `claude` via `tokio::process::Command`. Pipes prompt to stdin. Reads stdout line-by-line via `BufReader::lines()`. Parses each line as `StreamEvent` (stream-json format) extracting `Assistant` messages (text + tool_use), `Result` events (final output + token usage), and `session_id` for `--resume`. Runs an elapsed-time UI updater task. Waits for process exit. On `--resume` failure, retries fresh. |
    26вҶ’| **Calling context** | `run_iteration_with_context()` вҖ” the inner loop of sequential phase execution. Called once per iteration per phase. |
    27вҶ’| **Daemon API** | `SubmitRun` with phase/iteration context, then `AttachRun` / `StreamEvents` for the authoritative live event log. `StreamTaskOutput` remains available as a focused task-output view. Session resume becomes daemon-managed task-node retry. |
    28вҶ’| **Complexity** | **High** вҖ” This is the core execution loop. It manages: session resume/retry logic, StreamEvent parsing for UI feedback (tool_use, thinking, text), token usage extraction, signal parsing (`<promise>DONE</promise>`), and elapsed-time UI updates. The daemon must own iteration lifecycle, session state, and retry policy. |
    29вҶ’| **Notes** | The `--resume` fallback retry (line 691) is particularly tricky вҖ” the daemon must internalize this. Token usage extraction (re-parsing the result JSON) should become a first-class daemon event field. |
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’---
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’### A2. Swarm Executor вҖ” Parallel Agent Execution
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’| Field | Value |
    36вҶ’|-------|-------|
    37вҶ’| **File** | `src/swarm/executor.rs` |
    38вҶ’| **Line** | 323 (Command::new), 343 (spawn) |
    39вҶ’| **Subsystem** | Swarm (parallel DAG execution via `forge swarm`) |
    40вҶ’| **Current pattern** | Spawns `claude` with `--print --output-format stream-json`. Pipes prompt to stdin. Reads stdout via a dedicated `read_stdout()` task (line 527) that accumulates all output. Coordinates with a callback server (HTTP) for swarm events and a timeout task via `tokio::select!`. Parses completion signals from accumulated output (`parse_swarm_completion`). |
    41вҶ’| **Calling context** | `run_claude_process()` вҖ” called per-agent in the swarm DAG. Multiple instances run concurrently. |
    42вҶ’| **Daemon API** | `SubmitRun` as a coordinator task or `CreateChildTask` for each DAG node. The daemon's run graph replaces the DAG scheduler. `AttachRun` / `StreamEvents` replace stdout accumulation at the run level, and `StreamTaskOutput` is available for focused task tails. Callback server is replaced by daemon message bus. |
    43вҶ’| **Complexity** | **High** вҖ” Multi-task coordination: stdout reader, callback poller, timeout watcher all via `tokio::select!`. The callback server (HTTP) for swarm events is an entire subsystem that gets replaced by the daemon's message bus. Swarm completion parsing from stdout must become structured daemon events. |
    44вҶ’| **Notes** | The `CallbackServer` (swarm/callback.rs) is a bespoke HTTP server that agents post progress to. This entire pattern is subsumed by the daemon's message bus and task-node status updates. |
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’---
    47вҶ’
    48вҶ’### A3. Factory Agent Executor вҖ” Multi-Agent Task Execution
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’| Field | Value |
    51вҶ’|-------|-------|
    52вҶ’| **File** | `src/factory/agent_executor.rs` |
    53вҶ’| **Line** | 286 (Command::new), 307 (spawn) |
    54вҶ’| **Subsystem** | Factory (agent-per-task execution for kanban issues) |
    55вҶ’| **Current pattern** | Spawns `claude --print --verbose --dangerously-skip-permissions --output-format stream-json -p <description>`. Reads stdout via `BufReader::lines()`. Uses `OutputParser::parse_line()` which delegates to `parse_stream_json_line()` and also extracts signal tags (`<progress>`, `<blocker>`, `<pivot>`). Routes parsed events to WebSocket broadcasts (`WsMessage::AgentThinking`, `AgentAction`, `AgentSignal`, `AgentOutput`). Batches DB writes via a channel-based event writer task. Captures stderr after stdout closes. Stores child process handle in `running` map for cancellation. |
    56вҶ’| **Calling context** | `run_task()` вҖ” executes a single agent task within a Factory run. Multiple tasks may run concurrently with worktree isolation. |
    57вҶ’| **Daemon API** | `CreateChildTask` per agent task. `AttachRun` / `StreamEvents` replace stdout parsing as the authoritative event source. `StreamTaskOutput` remains a task-scoped projection. Daemon events replace WebSocket broadcasts (Factory UI subscribes to daemon event stream). |
    58вҶ’| **Complexity** | **High** вҖ” Most complex stdout parsing pipeline: signal extraction, stream-json parsing, file-change detection from tool_use metadata, WebSocket event routing, batched DB persistence, and process handle management for cancellation. All of this becomes daemon-side event processing. |
    59вҶ’| **Notes** | The `OutputParser` (lines 69-168) and `parse_stream_json_line()` are shared with pipeline execution. These parsers become unnecessary when the daemon emits structured events directly. The `AgentHandle` with process handle for cancellation maps to `KillTask` on the daemon. |
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’---
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’### A4. Review Dispatcher вҖ” Specialist Reviews
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’| Field | Value |
    66вҶ’|-------|-------|
    67вҶ’| **File** | `src/review/dispatcher.rs` |
    68вҶ’| **Line** | 490 (Command::new), 509 (spawn) |
    69вҶ’| **Subsystem** | Review (specialist code reviews: security, performance, architecture, simplicity) |
    70вҶ’| **Current pattern** | Spawns `claude --print` with `--allowed-tools Read,Glob,Grep,WebSearch,WebFetch` (read-only restriction). Pipes review prompt to stdin. Reads stdout via `BufReader::lines()`, accumulating raw text. Waits with timeout (`review_timeout`). No stream-json parsing вҖ” collects plain text output. |
    71вҶ’| **Calling context** | `run_claude_review()` вҖ” called per specialist type during the review gate after a phase completes. |
    72вҶ’| **Daemon API** | `CreateChildTask` with a review profile (read-only tools). `StreamTaskOutput` or `GetTask` plus task artifacts/result metadata for the review output. |
    73вҶ’| **Complexity** | **Medium** вҖ” Straightforward spawn-collect-parse pattern. The `--allowed-tools` restriction maps to daemon profile permissions. Timeout handling moves to daemon task-level timeout. |
    74вҶ’| **Notes** | The review prompt construction (`build_review_prompt`, line 579) stays client-side. Only the execution moves to the daemon. |
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’---
    77вҶ’
    78вҶ’### A5. Review Arbiter вҖ” LLM-Based Conflict Resolution
    79вҶ’
    80вҶ’| Field | Value |
    81вҶ’|-------|-------|
    82вҶ’| **File** | `src/review/arbiter.rs` |
    83вҶ’| **Line** | 1116 (Command::new), 1133 (spawn) |
    84вҶ’| **Subsystem** | Review (arbiter for gating failure resolution) |

   392вҶ’

   397вҶ’| `src/stream/mod.rs` | `StreamEvent` enum (assistant, user, result, system) from stream-json | Orchestrator runner (A1), Council worker (A7) | Daemon emits these as native event types |
   398вҶ’| `src/factory/pipeline/parsing.rs` | `StreamJsonEvent` (text, tool_start, tool_end, thinking) + `ProgressInfo` + `PhaseEvent` | Pipeline execution (A8/B2), Agent executor (A3) | Daemon event stream replaces all of this |
   399вҶ’| `src/factory/agent_executor.rs:69-168` | `OutputParser` вҖ” signal tags + stream-json delegation + file-change extraction | Agent executor (A3) | Subsumed by daemon structured events |

   404вҶ’## Migration Priority Order
   405вҶ’
   406вҶ’Based on spec Section 14.1 (additive, staged refactor):
   407вҶ’
   408вҶ’### Phase 1: Shared Execution Facade (Pre-daemon)
   409вҶ’1. Create `AgentExecutionFacade` trait behind which all spawn sites converge
   410вҶ’2. Migrate A1 (orchestrator), A2 (swarm), A3 (factory agent), A4 (review), A8 (pipeline) to use the facade

## 1. DESIGN SPEC OVERVIEW
**File:** `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-13-forge-runtime-platform-design.md` (70.3KB)

The spec is comprehensive and structured in 14 sections:
- **Problem:** Forge currently spawns agents directly; lacks isolation, shared services, inter-agent communication, dynamic team composition, declarative environments, security boundaries
- **Solution:** Single-node self-hosted runtime daemon with Nix-cached profiles, namespace isolation, rich sidecar services, message bus, approval gates
- **Key Architecture:**
  - Unix domain socket gRPC API
  - Trusted base profiles + project overlays (never executed in control plane)
  - Runtime backends: bubblewrap (Linux), Docker (macOS primary), host (explicit insecure)
  - Approval gates (soft caps вҶ’ parent-approvable, hard caps вҶ’ denied)
  - Memory scopes: Scratch (agent-local), RunShared (lane-scoped append-only), Project (durable)
  - Task-node centric execution (survives retries, failover, daemon restarts)
  - Sidecar services: auth proxy, credential broker, memory service, cache, MCP router, message bus
  - Comprehensive observability: event log replay, metrics, audit trail

## 2. GRPC CONTRACT (`runtime.proto`)

## 5. SPAWN SITE MIGRATION CHECKLIST
**File:** `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-13-spawn-site-migration-checklist.md`

### **Summary Statistics:**
- **Total spawn sites:** 26
  - Claude CLI spawns (Part A): 14 вҖ” **Complexity: 4 High, 4 Medium, 6 Low**
  - Forge subprocess spawns (Part B): 3 вҖ” **Complexity: 0 High, 1 Medium, 2 Low**
  - Git/Shell spawns (Part C): 8 вҖ” **Complexity: 0 High, 3 Medium, 3 Low, 2 N/A test**
  - Docker/Container (Part D): 1 вҖ” **Complexity: 0 High, 1 Medium**

### **High Complexity Spawn Sites (4):**

**A1. Orchestrator Runner** (`src/orchestrator/runner.rs` lines 511, 559)
- **Subsystem:** Sequential `forge run`
- **Current:** Spawns `claude` via tokio::process::Command, pipes prompt to stdin, reads stdout line-by-line as StreamEvent (stream-json), parses Assistant (text+tool_use), Result (output+token), session_id. Runs elapsed-time UI updater. Waits for exit. Retries with `--resume` fallback.
- **Daemon API:** SubmitRun вҶ’ AttachRun / StreamEvents (authoritative event log)
- **Complexity reasons:** Core execution loop, session resume/retry logic, StreamEvent parsing for UI (tool_use, thinking, text), token usage extraction, signal parsing (<promise>DONE</promise>), elapsed-time UI updates

**A2. Swarm Executor** (`src/swarm/executor.rs` lines 323, 343)
- **Subsystem:** Parallel DAG `forge swarm`
- **Current:** Spawns `claude --print --output-format stream-json`, pipes prompt, reads via dedicated `read_stdout()` task accumulating output. Coordinates with callback server (HTTP) and timeout via tokio::select!. Parses completion from accumulated output.
- **Daemon API:** SubmitRun as coordinator or CreateChildTask per DAG node, AttachRun/StreamEvents, StreamTaskOutput for task tails, message bus replaces callback server
- **Complexity reasons:** Multi-task coordination (stdout reader, callback poller, timeout watcher), CallbackServer (HTTP) is entire subsystem replaced by message bus, swarm completion parsing becomes structured events

**A3. Factory Agent Executor** (`src/factory/agent_executor.rs` lines 286, 307)
- **Subsystem:** Factory kanban task execution
- **Current:** Spawns `claude --print --verbose --dangerously-skip-permissions --output-format stream-json -p <desc>`, reads via BufReader::lines(), OutputParser::parse_line() delegates to parse_stream_json_line(), extracts signal tags (<progress>, <blocker>, <pivot>), routes parsed events to WebSocket (WsMessage::AgentThinking, AgentAction, AgentSignal, AgentOutput), batches DB writes via channel, captures stderr, stores process handle for cancellation
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask per agent task, AttachRun/StreamEvents (authoritative source), StreamTaskOutput (task-scoped), daemon events replace WebSocket broadcasts
- **Complexity reasons:** Most complex stdout parsing: signal extraction, stream-json parsing, file-change detection from tool metadata, WebSocket routing, batched DB persistence, process handle management for cancellation

**A8. Factory Pipeline Execution** (`src/factory/pipeline/execution.rs` lines 116, 144)
- **Subsystem:** Factory pipeline (fallback when no phases.json)
- **Current:** build_execution_command() returns forge swarm or claude (--print --verbose --output-format stream-json), reads lines, parses PhaseEvent (forge swarm) then ProgressInfo JSON then StreamJsonEvent (direct claude), emits WsMessage::PipelineProgress, PipelineOutputEvent, tool events, file changes via WebSocket
- **Daemon API:** SubmitRun for entire pipeline, daemon events replace stdout parsing, Factory UI subscribes to daemon stream
- **Complexity reasons:** Dual parsing path (swarm vs claude), complex WebSocket routing, file change detection from tool metadata, primary integration point (spec Section 7.6), RunHandle::Process stored for cancellation maps to StopRun

### **Medium Complexity Spawn Sites (9):**

**A4. Review Dispatcher** (`src/review/dispatcher.rs` lines 490, 509)
- **Subsystem:** Specialist code reviews
- **Current:** Spawns `claude --print --allowed-tools Read,Glob,Grep,WebSearch,WebFetch`, pipes review prompt, reads BufReader::lines() as plain text, waits with timeout
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask with review profile (read-only), StreamTaskOutput or GetTask for result metadata
- **Complexity:** Straightforward spawn-collect, --allowed-tools maps to profile permissions, timeout вҶ’ daemon task-level

**A5. Review Arbiter** (`src/review/arbiter.rs` lines 1116, 1133)
- **Subsystem:** LLM-based conflict resolution
- **Current:** Spawns `claude --print` with read-only tools, pipes arbiter prompt, reads BufReader::lines(), parses parse_arbiter_response() for structured JSON decision
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask with arbiter profile, GetTask for decision metadata
- **Complexity:** Similar to A4 but with structured response parsing

**A7. Council Worker (Claude & Codex)** (`src/council/worker.rs` lines 228, 628)
- **Subsystem:** Parallel deliberation
- **Current:** ClaudeWorker and CodexWorker both use run_command() which Command::new(&cmd).args(args).output().await. ClaudeWorker parses stream-json via parse_stream_output(). CodexWorker parses via parse_codex_output(). Both extract text + token usage. Multiple workers run concurrently in separate worktrees.
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask per council member, worktree already managed, daemon emits structured events, only council-specific interpretation stays client-side
- **Complexity:** Two worker types with different parsers, Worker trait abstraction, token usage preservation, CodexWorker supports alternative model backends

**B2. Factory Pipeline вҖ” forge swarm** (`src/factory/pipeline/execution.rs` line 98)
- **Subsystem:** Factory DAG-parallel
- **Current:** build_execution_command() returns `forge swarm --max-parallel 4 --fail-fast`, stdout streamed, parsed for PhaseEvent + progress JSON
- **Daemon API:** SubmitRun with full run plan
- **Complexity:** Forge-spawns-forge eliminated, daemon is single orchestrator, PhaseEvent parsing replaced by daemon event subscription

**C3. Factory Agent Executor вҖ” Worktree** (`src/factory/agent_executor.rs` lines 216, 244, 618-692)
- **Subsystem:** Git worktree isolation
- **Current:** `git worktree add/remove`, merge operations (rev-parse, checkout, merge, merge --abort) with conflict detection and rollback
- **Daemon API:** Daemon workspace coordinator (spec Section 8.3)
- **Complexity:** Merge logic (lines 616-701) has careful error handling + rollback, must be preserved

**C6. Council Merge** (`src/council/merge.rs` lines 36, 63, 122, 143, 347, 365)
- **Subsystem:** Worktree + patch operations
- **Current:** std::process::Command (sync). git worktree add/remove/prune, git diff, git apply with stdin pipe. run_git_with_input() pipes to git apply.
- **Daemon API:** Workspace coordinator for worktree ops, patch application stays utility
- **Complexity:** stdin piping for git apply slightly complex, currently sync, needs async

**C8. Hooks Executor вҖ” Shell Commands** (`src/hooks/executor.rs` line 130)
- **Subsystem:** User-defined hooks
- **Current:** Spawns `sh -c <command>` with FORGE_EVENT, FORGE_PHASE, FORGE_ITERATION env vars, pipes context JSON to stdin, waits with timeout, parses exit code
- **Daemon API:** Daemon executes hooks as task-node lifecycle, security: untrusted user-authored code
- **Complexity:** Env var injection + stdin piping, timeout handling, security model for untrusted code

**D1. Factory Sandbox вҖ” Docker** (`src/factory/sandbox.rs` lines 138, 270, 309, 344)
- **Subsystem:** Docker-based sandboxed execution
- **Current:** Uses bollard crate (Docker API, not Command::new). Creates containers, mounts volumes, streams logs via mpsc, manages lifecycle.
- **Daemon API:** DockerRuntime backend (spec 4.5, 4.6), AgentRuntime trait
- **Complexity:** Already well-structured, bollard maps to DockerRuntime, mpsc analogous to daemon event streaming

### **Low Complexity Spawn Sites (11):**

**A6. Hooks Executor вҖ” Prompt** (`src/hooks/executor.rs` line 246)
- **Subsystem:** Claude-based hook evaluation
- **Current:** Spawns `claude --print --no-session-persistence --dangerously-skip-permissions`, pipes prompt, reads stdout, parses JSON (action, message, inject)
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask with lightweight evaluator profile
- **Complexity:** Simple spawn-collect, no streaming, small JSON response

**A9. Factory Planner** (`src/factory/planner.rs` line 336)
- **Subsystem:** Issue analysis
- **Current:** Spawns `claude --print --output-format text -p <prompt>`, waits, parses text as JSON (agent task decomposition)
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask with planner profile
- **Complexity:** Fire-and-forget, no streaming

**A10. Implement Extract** (`src/implement/extract.rs` line 184)
- **Subsystem:** Design doc analysis
- **Current:** std::process::Command (blocking) `claude --print --no-session-persistence -p <prompt>`, waits, parses JSON (spec + phases)
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask with extraction profile
- **Complexity:** Sync (needs async conversion), no streaming

**A11. Generate** (`src/generate/mod.rs` line 186)
- **Subsystem:** Spec вҶ’ phases.json
- **Current:** std::process::Command (blocking) `claude --print --no-session-persistence -p <prompt>`, waits, parses JSON вҶ’ Vec<Phase>
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask with generator profile
- **Complexity:** Sync, no streaming

**A12. Interview** (`src/interview/mod.rs` lines 39, 406)
- **Subsystem:** Interactive spec gathering
- **Current:** build_interview_command() with `--print --dangerously-skip-permissions --system-prompt`, std::process::Command (sync), each turn spawns fresh (with --continue), collects stdout, parses <spec>...</spec> tags
- **Daemon API:** SubmitRun or CreateChildTask, session continuity via daemon state instead of --continue flag
- **Complexity:** Multi-turn loop with session state, CLI interactive I/O stays local, only Claude execution to daemon

**A13. Autoresearch Benchmark** (`src/cmd/autoresearch/runner.rs` line 195)
- **Subsystem:** Prompt benchmarking
- **Current:** tokio::process::Command(cmd.program).args(cmd.args).output().await, fire-and-forget
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask with benchmark profile
- **Complexity:** Simple fire-and-forget

**A14. Autoresearch Judge** (`src/cmd/autoresearch/judge.rs` line 154)
- **Subsystem:** Quality judgment
- **Current:** TokioExecutor::execute() trait, Command spawn + collect
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask with judge profile
- **Complexity:** Behind trait, easy to swap

**B1. Factory Pipeline вҖ” forge generate** (`src/factory/pipeline/execution.rs` line 66)
- **Subsystem:** Auto-generate phases
- **Current:** tokio::process::Command for `forge generate`, waits for status, checks if phases.json created
- **Daemon API:** SubmitRun with generate-phases task
- **Complexity:** Fire-and-forget, no stdout parsing

**B3. Factory API вҖ” forge --help** (`src/factory/api.rs` line 399)
- **Subsystem:** CLI help endpoint
- **Current:** std::process::Command (sync) `forge --help`, caches in OnceLock, parses output
- **Daemon API:** Could query daemon, but could stay as-is (static help cache)
- **Complexity:** Trivial, low priority UX feature

**C1, C2, C4, C5.** Git operations (branches, push, PR, clone, remote ops) вҖ” **Low, daemon workspace coordinator**

### **Migration Priority Order:**
1. **Phase 1 (Pre-daemon):** Create AgentExecutionFacade trait, converge A1, A2, A3, A4, A8
2. **Phase 2 (Core daemon, high complexity):** A1, A2, A3, A8/B2
3. **Phase 3 (Review + Council, medium):** A4, A5, A7, C6
4. **Phase 4 (Lightweight):** A6, A9, A10, A11, A12, A13/A14
5. **Phase 5 (Infrastructure):** C8, D1, C1-C5, B3, retire stream parsers

---

## 6. CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION NOTES

### **Type System Strengths:**
- Strong newtype IDs prevent accidental misuse (RunId vs TaskNodeId)
- BudgetEnvelope with consumed/subtree_consumed rollup and threshold helpers
- CompiledProfile cleanly separates trusted base profile + validated overlay
- RuntimeEnvPlan enum supports Nix, Docker, and explicit Host fallback
- ApprovalState and ApprovalReasonKind provide clear gate semantics

### **Event Log Design:**
- Monotonic `seq` for durable replay across daemon restarts
- RuntimeEventKind enum with 40+ variants covers entire lifecycle
- TaskOutputEvent cursor is authoritative position in event log
- BusMessage supports request/reply, broadcast, and child-task lifecycle

### **Policy Evaluation:**
- Layered: global вҶ’ project вҶ’ compiled manifest
- PolicyDecision enum: Approved, RequiresApproval, Denied
- Policies affect limits (tasks, depth, concurrency), credentials, network, memory, approval gates, costs

### **Migration Complexity Drivers:**
1. **Session resume logic** (A1) вҖ” daemon must own task-node retry policy
2. **Callback server replacement** (A2) вҖ” entire HTTP subsystem вҶ’ message bus
3. **Stdout parsing pipelines** (A3, A8) вҖ” stream-json, signal tags, file-change detection вҶ’ structured daemon events
4. **WebSocket integration** (A3, Factory UI) вҖ” daemon event subscription replaces direct process output routing
5. **Token usage tracking** вҖ” currently re-parsed from result JSON, daemon should emit as first-class field
6. **Worktree coordination** (C3, C6) вҖ” careful merge logic with rollback must move to daemon

### **Workspace Considerations:**
- `forge-common` is the shared type hub (no other crates)
- `forge-proto` generates gRPC bindings from runtime.proto
- Main `forge` binary contains all subsystems (orchestrator, swarm, factory, review, council, hooks, etc.)
- Docker integration via bollard (Docker API, not Command)
- No existing daemon crate; forge-runtime daemon will be new

---

## 7. DETAILED FILE PATHS & LOCATIONS

**Design spec:** `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-13-forge-runtime-platform-design.md`

**Proto contract:** `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/crates/forge-proto/proto/runtime.proto`

**Type definitions:**
- `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/crates/forge-common/src/lib.rs` (module root)
- `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/crates/forge-common/src/ids.rs` (7 ID types)
- `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/crates/forge-common/src/manifest.rs` (13 types + CompiledProfile, AgentManifest, RuntimeEnvPlan)
- `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/crates/forge-common/src/run_graph.rs` (20+ types, RunGraph, RunState, TaskNode, approval types)
- `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/crates/forge-common/src/policy.rs` (Policy, 5 policy sub-types, PolicyDecision)
- `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/crates/forge-common/src/events.rs` (RuntimeEvent, 40+ RuntimeEventKind variants, BusMessage, TaskOutputEvent, TaskOutput)
- `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/crates/forge-common/src/runtime.rs` (AgentRuntime trait, AgentStatus)

**Dependencies:** `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/crates/forge-common/Cargo.toml`, `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/Cargo.toml`

**Spawn site checklist:** `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-13-spawn-site-migration-checklist.md`

---

This is your complete technical reference for implementation planning. All 26 spawn sites are mapped to complexity tiers, daemon API replacements, and migration priorities. Type signatures, trait methods, and enum variants are fully documented for seamless integration work.

Perfect. Now let me create a comprehensive implementation planning document for you.

## Complete Implementation Planning Details for Forge Runtime Platform

I've read all 12 files thoroughly. Here's the complete technical breakdown:

---

## 1. DESIGN SPEC OVERVIEW
**File:** `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-13-forge-runtime-platform-design.md` (70.3KB)

The spec is comprehensive and structured in 14 sections:
- **Problem:** Forge currently spawns agents directly; lacks isolation, shared services, inter-agent communication, dynamic team composition, declarative environments, security boundaries
- **Solution:** Single-node self-hosted runtime daemon with Nix-cached profiles, namespace isolation, rich sidecar services, message bus, approval gates
- **Key Architecture:**
  - Unix domain socket gRPC API
  - Trusted base profiles + project overlays (never executed in control plane)
  - Runtime backends: bubblewrap (Linux), Docker (macOS primary), host (explicit insecure)
  - Approval gates (soft caps вҶ’ parent-approvable, hard caps вҶ’ denied)
  - Memory scopes: Scratch (agent-local), RunShared (lane-scoped append-only), Project (durable)
  - Task-node centric execution (survives retries, failover, daemon restarts)
  - Sidecar services: auth proxy, credential broker, memory service, cache, MCP router, message bus
  - Comprehensive observability: event log replay, metrics, audit trail

---

## 2. GRPC CONTRACT (`runtime.proto`)
**File:** `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/crates/forge-proto/proto/runtime.proto`

### Service RPCs (19 methods):
1. **Run Lifecycle:**
   - `SubmitRun(SubmitRunRequest) вҶ’ RunInfo`
   - `AttachRun(AttachRunRequest) вҶ’ stream RuntimeEvent`
   - `StopRun(StopRunRequest) вҶ’ RunInfo`
   - `GetRun(GetRunRequest) вҶ’ RunInfo`
   - `ListRuns(ListRunsRequest) вҶ’ ListRunsResponse`

2. **Task Lifecycle:**
   - `GetTask(GetTaskRequest) вҶ’ TaskInfo`
   - `ListTasks(ListTasksRequest) вҶ’ ListTasksResponse`
   - `StreamTaskOutput(StreamTaskOutputRequest) вҶ’ stream TaskOutputEvent`
   - `CreateChildTask(CreateChildTaskRequest) вҶ’ CreateChildTaskResponse`
   - `KillTask(KillTaskRequest) вҶ’ KillTaskResponse`

3. **Approval Flow:**
   - `PendingApprovals(PendingApprovalsRequest) вҶ’ stream ApprovalRequest`
   - `ResolveApproval(ResolveApprovalRequest) вҶ’ ResolveApprovalResponse`

4. **MCP Service Management:**
   - `RegisterMcpServer(RegisterMcpServerRequest) вҶ’ McpServerInfo`
   - `ListMcpServers(ListMcpServersRequest) вҶ’ ListMcpServersResponse`

5. **Observability:**
   - `StreamEvents(StreamEventsRequest) вҶ’ stream RuntimeEvent`
   - `GetMetrics(GetMetricsRequest) вҶ’ MetricsSnapshot`

6. **Daemon Management:**
   - `Health(HealthRequest) вҶ’ HealthResponse`
   - `Shutdown(ShutdownRequest) вҶ’ ShutdownResponse`

### Key Proto Enums (21):
- **RunStatus:** SUBMITTED, SCHEDULING, RUNNING, PAUSED, COMPLETED, FAILED, CANCELLED
- **TaskStatus:** PENDING, AWAITING_APPROVAL, ENQUEUED, MATERIALIZING, RUNNING, COMPLETED, FAILED, KILLED
- **RuntimeBackend:** BWRAP, DOCKER, HOST
- **ApprovalAction:** APPROVE, DENY
- **ApprovalActorKind:** PARENT_TASK, OPERATOR, AUTO
- **ApprovalReasonKind:** SOFT_CAP_EXCEEDED, CAPABILITY_ESCALATION, BUDGET_EXCEPTION, PROFILE_APPROVAL, MEMORY_PROMOTION, INSECURE_RUNTIME_RESTRICTION
- **ApprovalState:** NOT_REQUIRED, PENDING, APPROVED, DENIED
- **ApprovalMode:** AUTO_WITHIN_ENVELOPE, PARENT_WITHIN_ENVELOPE, OPERATOR_REQUIRED
- **MemoryScope:** SCRATCH, RUN_SHARED, PROJECT
- **RunSharedWriteMode:** APPEND_ONLY_LANE, COORDINATED_SHARED_WRITE
- **RepoAccess:** NONE, READ_ONLY, READ_WRITE
- **CredentialAccessMode:** PROXY_ONLY, EXPORTABLE
- **TaskWaitMode:** ASYNC, WAIT_FOR_COMPLETION
- **MilestoneStatus:** PENDING, RUNNING, BLOCKED, COMPLETED, FAILED

### Key Proto Messages (Complex structures):
- `SubmitRunRequest`: project, plan (RunPlan), workspace, policy_overrides, runtime_backend, idempotency_key, client_identity
- `RunInfo`: id, project, status, milestones[], task_count, token_usage, estimated_cost_usd, runtime_backend, timestamps
- `RunPlan`: version, milestones[], initial_tasks[], global_budget
- `MilestonePlan`: id, title, objective, expected_output, depends_on[], success_criteria[], default_profile, budget, approval_mode
- `TaskInfo`: id, run_id, parent_task_id, milestone_id, objective, expected_output, profile, budget, memory_scope, status, assigned_agent_id, children[], resource_snapshot, tokens, approval_state, capabilities, wait_mode, result_summary
- `BudgetEnvelope`: max_tokens, max_duration, max_children, require_approval_after, max_depth
- `ResourceSnapshot`: agent_id, task_id, cpu_usage, memory_bytes, disk_bytes, network_egress_bytes, network_ingress_bytes
- `AgentManifest`: profile_name, tools[], mcp_servers[], credentials[], memory_policy, resource_limits, permissions
- `MemoryPolicy`: read_scopes[], write_scopes[], run_shared_write_mode
- `PermissionSet`: repo_access, network_allowlist[], spawn (max_children, require_approval_after), allow_project_memory_promotion
- `CapabilityEnvelope`: tools[], mcp_servers[], credentials[], network_allowlist[], memory_policy, repo_access, spawn, allow_project_memory_promotion
- `RuntimeEvent`: sequence, run_id, task_id, agent_id, timestamp, event (enum oneof with 8 variants: RunStatusChangedEvent, TaskStatusChangedEvent, TaskOutputEvent, ApprovalRequest, ResourceSnapshot, ServiceEvent, MemoryEvent)
- `TaskOutputEvent`: run_id, task_id, agent_id, cursor, timestamp, event (oneof: StdoutLine, Signal, Promise, TokenUsageDelta)

---

## 3. RUST TYPE ECOSYSTEM

### **forge-common Crate Structure**
**File:** `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/crates/forge-common/src/lib.rs`

Re-exports from 5 modules: `events`, `ids`, `manifest`, `policy`, `run_graph`, `runtime`

**Dependencies** (Cargo.toml):
- serde (1) + serde_json
- chrono (0.4) with serde
- uuid (1) with v4, serde
- async-trait (0.1)
- anyhow (1)
- thiserror (2)

### **IDs Module** (`ids.rs`)
Uses macro-defined newtype wrappers (each has Display, From<String>, From<&str>, AsRef<str>, serde support):
- `RunId` вҖ” entire run execution
- `TaskNodeId` вҖ” durable unit of work within a run
- `AgentId` вҖ” concrete worker process/container
- `MilestoneId` вҖ” operator-facing phase marker
- `ApprovalId` вҖ” pending approval request
- `SpawnId` вҖ” pending child-task spawn awaiting approval
- `ChannelId` вҖ” message bus channel for task-to-task communication

Methods: `new(impl Into<String>)`, `generate() вҶ’ Self` (UUID v4), `as_str() вҶ’ &str`, `into_inner() вҶ’ String`

### **Manifest Module** (`manifest.rs`)
Core types for agent capability and environment definition:

**CompiledProfile:**
- base_profile: String
- overlay_hash: Option<String> (cache key)
- manifest: AgentManifest
- env_plan: RuntimeEnvPlan

**AgentManifest:**
- name: String
- tools: Vec<String>
- mcp_servers: Vec<String>
- credentials: Vec<CredentialGrant>
- memory_policy: MemoryPolicy
- resources: ResourceLimits
- permissions: PermissionSet

**RuntimeEnvPlan (enum):**
- **NixProfile:** store_path (PathBuf), flake_ref (String)
- **Docker:** image (String), extra_mounts (Vec<DockerMount>)
- **Host:** explicit_opt_in (bool)

**MemoryScope (enum):**
- Scratch (agent-local)
- RunShared (lane-scoped append-only)
- Project (cross-run durable)

**MemoryPolicy:**
- read_scopes: Vec<MemoryScope>
- write_scopes: Vec<MemoryScope>
- run_shared_write_mode: RunSharedWriteMode

**RunSharedWriteMode (enum):**
- AppendOnlyLane
- CoordinatedSharedWrite

**CredentialAccessMode (enum):**
- ProxyOnly (default)
- Exportable (requires approval)

**CredentialGrant:**
- handle: String
- access_mode: CredentialAccessMode

**ResourceLimits:**
- cpu: f32 (fractional cores)
- memory_bytes: u64
- token_budget: u64

**PermissionSet:**
- repo_access: RepoAccess
- network_allowlist: HashSet<String>
- spawn_limits: SpawnLimits
- allow_project_memory_promotion: bool

**RepoAccess (enum):**
- None
- ReadOnly
- ReadWrite

**SpawnLimits:**
- max_children: u32 (hard cap)
- require_approval_after: u32 (soft cap)

**CapabilityEnvelope:**
- tools: Vec<String>
- mcp_servers: Vec<String>
- credentials: Vec<CredentialGrant>
- network_allowlist: HashSet<String>
- memory_policy: MemoryPolicy
- repo_access: RepoAccess
- spawn_limits: SpawnLimits
- allow_project_memory_promotion: bool

**BudgetEnvelope:**
- allocated: u64 (total tokens)
- consumed: u64 (direct consumption)
- subtree_consumed: u64 (self + children)
- remaining: u64 (convenience field)
- warn_at_percent: u8
- **Methods:** `new(allocated, warn_at_percent)`, `consume(tokens) вҶ’ bool` (returns true if exhausted), `consume_subtree(tokens)`, `is_warning_threshold_reached() вҶ’ bool`, `is_exhausted() вҶ’ bool`

**WorktreePlan (enum):**
- **Dedicated:** worktree_path, branch
- **Shared:** workspace_path
- **Isolated:** worktree_path, branch

### **Run Graph Module** (`run_graph.rs`)

**RunGraph:**
- runs: HashMap<RunId, RunState>
- **Methods:** `new()`, `insert_run()`, `get_run()`, `get_run_mut()`

**RunPlan:**
- version: u32
- milestones: Vec<MilestoneInfo>
- initial_tasks: Vec<TaskTemplate>
- global_budget: BudgetEnvelope

**TaskTemplate:**
- milestone: MilestoneId
- objective: String
- expected_output: String
- profile_hint: String
- budget: BudgetEnvelope
- memory_scope: MemoryScope
- depends_on: Vec<TaskNodeId>

**RunState:**
- id: RunId
- project: String
- plan: RunPlan
- milestones: HashMap<MilestoneId, MilestoneState>
- tasks: HashMap<TaskNodeId, TaskNode>
- approvals: HashMap<ApprovalId, PendingApproval>
- status: RunStatus
- last_event_cursor: u64
- submitted_at, finished_at: DateTime<Utc>
- total_tokens: u64
- estimated_cost_usd: f64
- **Methods:** 
  - `get_ready_tasks() вҶ’ Vec<TaskNodeId>` вҖ” filters Pending tasks with completed dependencies
  - `get_task_subtree_budget(&TaskNodeId) вҶ’ u64` вҖ” recursive rollup
  - `add_child_task(&parent_id, child) вҶ’ Result<(), RunGraphError>`
  - `update_task_status(&task_id, status) вҶ’ Result<(), RunGraphError>`
  - `assign_agent(&task_id, agent_id) вҶ’ Result<(), RunGraphError>`

**TaskNode:**
- id: TaskNodeId
- parent_task: Option<TaskNodeId>
- milestone: MilestoneId
- depends_on: Vec<TaskNodeId>
- objective: String
- expected_output: String
- profile: CompiledProfile
- budget: BudgetEnvelope
- memory_scope: MemoryScope
- approval_state: ApprovalState
- requested_capabilities: CapabilityEnvelope
- wait_mode: TaskWaitMode
- worktree: WorktreePlan
- assigned_agent: Option<AgentId>
- children: Vec<TaskNodeId>
- result_summary: Option<TaskResultSummary>
- status: TaskStatus
- created_at, finished_at: DateTime<Utc>

**TaskStatus (enum):**
- Pending
- AwaitingApproval
- Enqueued
- Materializing
- **Running { agent_id: AgentId, since: DateTime<Utc> }**
- **Completed { result: AgentResult, duration: chrono::Duration }**

## 5. SPAWN SITE MIGRATION CHECKLIST
**File:** `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-13-spawn-site-migration-checklist.md`

### **Summary Statistics:**
- **Total spawn sites:** 26
  - Claude CLI spawns (Part A): 14 вҖ” **Complexity: 4 High, 4 Medium, 6 Low**
  - Forge subprocess spawns (Part B): 3 вҖ” **Complexity: 0 High, 1 Medium, 2 Low**
  - Git/Shell spawns (Part C): 8 вҖ” **Complexity: 0 High, 3 Medium, 3 Low, 2 N/A test**
  - Docker/Container (Part D): 1 вҖ” **Complexity: 0 High, 1 Medium**

### **High Complexity Spawn Sites (4):**

**A1. Orchestrator Runner** (`src/orchestrator/runner.rs` lines 511, 559)
- **Subsystem:** Sequential `forge run`
- **Current:** Spawns `claude` via tokio::process::Command, pipes prompt to stdin, reads stdout line-by-line as StreamEvent (stream-json), parses Assistant (text+tool_use), Result (output+token), session_id. Runs elapsed-time UI updater. Waits for exit. Retries with `--resume` fallback.
- **Daemon API:** SubmitRun вҶ’ AttachRun / StreamEvents (authoritative event log)
- **Complexity reasons:** Core execution loop, session resume/retry logic, StreamEvent parsing for UI (tool_use, thinking, text), token usage extraction, signal parsing (<promise>DONE</promise>), elapsed-time UI updates

**A2. Swarm Executor** (`src/swarm/executor.rs` lines 323, 343)
- **Subsystem:** Parallel DAG `forge swarm`
- **Current:** Spawns `claude --print --output-format stream-json`, pipes prompt, reads via dedicated `read_stdout()` task accumulating output. Coordinates with callback server (HTTP) and timeout via tokio::select!. Parses completion from accumulated output.
- **Daemon API:** SubmitRun as coordinator or CreateChildTask per DAG node, AttachRun/StreamEvents, StreamTaskOutput for task tails, message bus replaces callback server
- **Complexity reasons:** Multi-task coordination (stdout reader, callback poller, timeout watcher), CallbackServer (HTTP) is entire subsystem replaced by message bus, swarm completion parsing becomes structured events

**A3. Factory Agent Executor** (`src/factory/agent_executor.rs` lines 286, 307)
- **Subsystem:** Factory kanban task execution
- **Current:** Spawns `claude --print --verbose --dangerously-skip-permissions --output-format stream-json -p <desc>`, reads via BufReader::lines(), OutputParser::parse_line() delegates to parse_stream_json_line(), extracts signal tags (<progress>, <blocker>, <pivot>), routes parsed events to WebSocket (WsMessage::AgentThinking, AgentAction, AgentSignal, AgentOutput), batches DB writes via channel, captures stderr, stores process handle for cancellation
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask per agent task, AttachRun/StreamEvents (authoritative source), StreamTaskOutput (task-scoped), daemon events replace WebSocket broadcasts
- **Complexity reasons:** Most complex stdout parsing: signal extraction, stream-json parsing, file-change detection from tool metadata, WebSocket routing, batched DB persistence, process handle management for cancellation

**A8. Factory Pipeline Execution** (`src/factory/pipeline/execution.rs` lines 116, 144)
- **Subsystem:** Factory pipeline (fallback when no phases.json)
- **Current:** build_execution_command() returns forge swarm or claude (--print --verbose --output-format stream-json), reads lines, parses PhaseEvent (forge swarm) then ProgressInfo JSON then StreamJsonEvent (direct claude), emits WsMessage::PipelineProgress, PipelineOutputEvent, tool events, file changes via WebSocket
- **Daemon API:** SubmitRun for entire pipeline, daemon events replace stdout parsing, Factory UI subscribes to daemon stream
- **Complexity reasons:** Dual parsing path (swarm vs claude), complex WebSocket routing, file change detection from tool metadata, primary integration point (spec Section 7.6), RunHandle::Process stored for cancellation maps to StopRun

### **Medium Complexity Spawn Sites (9):**

**A4. Review Dispatcher** (`src/review/dispatcher.rs` lines 490, 509)
- **Subsystem:** Specialist code reviews
- **Current:** Spawns `claude --print --allowed-tools Read,Glob,Grep,WebSearch,WebFetch`, pipes review prompt, reads BufReader::lines() as plain text, waits with timeout
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask with review profile (read-only), StreamTaskOutput or GetTask for result metadata
- **Complexity:** Straightforward spawn-collect, --allowed-tools maps to profile permissions, timeout вҶ’ daemon task-level

**A5. Review Arbiter** (`src/review/arbiter.rs` lines 1116, 1133)
- **Subsystem:** LLM-based conflict resolution
- **Current:** Spawns `claude --print` with read-only tools, pipes arbiter prompt, reads BufReader::lines(), parses parse_arbiter_response() for structured JSON decision
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask with arbiter profile, GetTask for decision metadata
- **Complexity:** Similar to A4 but with structured response parsing

**A7. Council Worker (Claude & Codex)** (`src/council/worker.rs` lines 228, 628)
- **Subsystem:** Parallel deliberation
- **Current:** ClaudeWorker and CodexWorker both use run_command() which Command::new(&cmd).args(args).output().await. ClaudeWorker parses stream-json via parse_stream_output(). CodexWorker parses via parse_codex_output(). Both extract text + token usage. Multiple workers run concurrently in separate worktrees.
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask per council member, worktree already managed, daemon emits structured events, only council-specific interpretation stays client-side
- **Complexity:** Two worker types with different parsers, Worker trait abstraction, token usage preservation, CodexWorker supports alternative model backends

**B2. Factory Pipeline вҖ” forge swarm** (`src/factory/pipeline/execution.rs` line 98)
- **Subsystem:** Factory DAG-parallel
- **Current:** build_execution_command() returns `forge swarm --max-parallel 4 --fail-fast`, stdout streamed, parsed for PhaseEvent + progress JSON
- **Daemon API:** SubmitRun with full run plan
- **Complexity:** Forge-spawns-forge eliminated, daemon is single orchestrator, PhaseEvent parsing replaced by daemon event subscription

**C3. Factory Agent Executor вҖ” Worktree** (`src/factory/agent_executor.rs` lines 216, 244, 618-692)
- **Subsystem:** Git worktree isolation
- **Current:** `git worktree add/remove`, merge operations (rev-parse, checkout, merge, merge --abort) with conflict detection and rollback
- **Daemon API:** Daemon workspace coordinator (spec Section 8.3)
- **Complexity:** Merge logic (lines 616-701) has careful error handling + rollback, must be preserved

**C6. Council Merge** (`src/council/merge.rs` lines 36, 63, 122, 143, 347, 365)
- **Subsystem:** Worktree + patch operations
- **Current:** std::process::Command (sync). git worktree add/remove/prune, git diff, git apply with stdin pipe. run_git_with_input() pipes to git apply.
- **Daemon API:** Workspace coordinator for worktree ops, patch application stays utility
- **Complexity:** stdin piping for git apply slightly complex, currently sync, needs async

**C8. Hooks Executor вҖ” Shell Commands** (`src/hooks/executor.rs` line 130)
- **Subsystem:** User-defined hooks
- **Current:** Spawns `sh -c <command>` with FORGE_EVENT, FORGE_PHASE, FORGE_ITERATION env vars, pipes context JSON to stdin, waits with timeout, parses exit code
- **Daemon API:** Daemon executes hooks as task-node lifecycle, security: untrusted user-authored code
- **Complexity:** Env var injection + stdin piping, timeout handling, security model for untrusted code

**D1. Factory Sandbox вҖ” Docker** (`src/factory/sandbox.rs` lines 138, 270, 309, 344)
- **Subsystem:** Docker-based sandboxed execution
- **Current:** Uses bollard crate (Docker API, not Command::new). Creates containers, mounts volumes, streams logs via mpsc, manages lifecycle.
- **Daemon API:** DockerRuntime backend (spec 4.5, 4.6), AgentRuntime trait
- **Complexity:** Already well-structured, bollard maps to DockerRuntime, mpsc analogous to daemon event streaming

### **Low Complexity Spawn Sites (11):**

**A6. Hooks Executor вҖ” Prompt** (`src/hooks/executor.rs` line 246)
- **Subsystem:** Claude-based hook evaluation
- **Current:** Spawns `claude --print --no-session-persistence --dangerously-skip-permissions`, pipes prompt, reads stdout, parses JSON (action, message, inject)
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask with lightweight evaluator profile
- **Complexity:** Simple spawn-collect, no streaming, small JSON response

**A9. Factory Planner** (`src/factory/planner.rs` line 336)
- **Subsystem:** Issue analysis
- **Current:** Spawns `claude --print --output-format text -p <prompt>`, waits, parses text as JSON (agent task decomposition)
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask with planner profile
- **Complexity:** Fire-and-forget, no streaming

**A10. Implement Extract** (`src/implement/extract.rs` line 184)
- **Subsystem:** Design doc analysis
- **Current:** std::process::Command (blocking) `claude --print --no-session-persistence -p <prompt>`, waits, parses JSON (spec + phases)
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask with extraction profile
- **Complexity:** Sync (needs async conversion), no streaming

**A11. Generate** (`src/generate/mod.rs` line 186)
- **Subsystem:** Spec вҶ’ phases.json
- **Current:** std::process::Command (blocking) `claude --print --no-session-persistence -p <prompt>`, waits, parses JSON вҶ’ Vec<Phase>
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask with generator profile
- **Complexity:** Sync, no streaming

**A12. Interview** (`src/interview/mod.rs` lines 39, 406)
- **Subsystem:** Interactive spec gathering
- **Current:** build_interview_command() with `--print --dangerously-skip-permissions --system-prompt`, std::process::Command (sync), each turn spawns fresh (with --continue), collects stdout, parses <spec>...</spec> tags
- **Daemon API:** SubmitRun or CreateChildTask, session continuity via daemon state instead of --continue flag
- **Complexity:** Multi-turn loop with session state, CLI interactive I/O stays local, only Claude execution to daemon

**A13. Autoresearch Benchmark** (`src/cmd/autoresearch/runner.rs` line 195)
- **Subsystem:** Prompt benchmarking
- **Current:** tokio::process::Command(cmd.program).args(cmd.args).output().await, fire-and-forget
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask with benchmark profile
- **Complexity:** Simple fire-and-forget

**A14. Autoresearch Judge** (`src/cmd/autoresearch/judge.rs` line 154)
- **Subsystem:** Quality judgment
- **Current:** TokioExecutor::execute() trait, Command spawn + collect
- **Daemon API:** CreateChildTask with judge profile
- **Complexity:** Behind trait, easy to swap

**B1. Factory Pipeline вҖ” forge generate** (`src/factory/pipeline/execution.rs` line 66)
- **Subsystem:** Auto-generate phases
- **Current:** tokio::process::Command for `forge generate`, waits for status, checks if phases.json created
- **Daemon API:** SubmitRun with generate-phases task
- **Complexity:** Fire-and-forget, no stdout parsing

**B3. Factory API вҖ” forge --help** (`src/factory/api.rs` line 399)
- **Subsystem:** CLI help endpoint
- **Current:** std::process::Command (sync) `forge --help`, caches in OnceLock, parses output
- **Daemon API:** Could query daemon, but could stay as-is (static help cache)
- **Complexity:** Trivial, low priority UX feature

**C1, C2, C4, C5.** Git operations (branches, push, PR, clone, remote ops) вҖ” **Low, daemon workspace coordinator**

### **Migration Priority Order:**
1. **Phase 1 (Pre-daemon):** Create AgentExecutionFacade trait, converge A1, A2, A3, A4, A8
2. **Phase 2 (Core daemon, high complexity):** A1, A2, A3, A8/B2
3. **Phase 3 (Review + Council, medium):** A4, A5, A7, C6
4. **Phase 4 (Lightweight):** A6, A9, A10, A11, A12, A13/A14
5. **Phase 5 (Infrastructure):** C8, D1, C1-C5, B3, retire stream parsers

## 6. CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION NOTES

### **Type System Strengths:**
- Strong newtype IDs prevent accidental misuse (RunId vs TaskNodeId)
- BudgetEnvelope with consumed/subtree_consumed rollup and threshold helpers
- CompiledProfile cleanly separates trusted base profile + validated overlay
- RuntimeEnvPlan enum supports Nix, Docker, and explicit Host fallback
- ApprovalState and ApprovalReasonKind provide clear gate semantics

### **Event Log Design:**
- Monotonic `seq` for durable replay across daemon restarts
- RuntimeEventKind enum with 40+ variants covers entire lifecycle
- TaskOutputEvent cursor is authoritative position in event log
- BusMessage supports request/reply, broadcast, and child-task lifecycle

### **Policy Evaluation:**
- Layered: global вҶ’ project вҶ’ compiled manifest
- PolicyDecision enum: Approved, RequiresApproval, Denied
- Policies affect limits (tasks, depth, concurrency), credentials, network, memory, approval gates, costs

### **Migration Complexity Drivers:**
1. **Session resume logic** (A1) вҖ” daemon must own task-node retry policy
2. **Callback server replacement** (A2) вҖ” entire HTTP subsystem вҶ’ message bus
3. **Stdout parsing pipelines** (A3, A8) вҖ” stream-json, signal tags, file-change detection вҶ’ structured daemon events
4. **WebSocket integration** (A3, Factory UI) вҖ” daemon event subscription replaces direct process output routing
5. **Token usage tracking** вҖ” currently re-parsed from result JSON, daemon should emit as first-class field
6. **Worktree coordination** (C3, C6) вҖ” careful merge logic with rollback must move to daemon

### **Workspace Considerations:**
- `forge-common` is the shared type hub (no other crates)
- `forge-proto` generates gRPC bindings from runtime.proto
- Main `forge` binary contains all subsystems (orchestrator, swarm, factory, review, council, hooks, etc.)
- Docker integration via bollard (Docker API, not Command)
- No existing daemon crate; forge-runtime daemon will be new

[package]
name = "forge"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"
description = "AI-powered development orchestrator - execute specs through sequential phases"

[dependencies]
# CLI
clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
dialoguer = "0.12"
console = "0.16"
indicatif = "0.18"

# Async
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full", "process"] }
futures = "0.3"
futures-util = "0.3"

# HTTP Server (for swarm callback server)
axum = { version = "0.8", features = ["ws"] }
tower-http = { version = "0.6", features = ["cors"] }
open = "5"
reqwest = { version = "0.13", features = ["json", "form", "query", "rustls"] }

# Git
git2 = "0.20.4"

# Serialization
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
serde_yaml = "0.9"

# Utilities
anyhow = "1"
thiserror = "2"
chrono = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde"] }
uuid = { version = "1", features = ["v4", "serde"] }
sha2 = "0.10"
walkdir = "2"
glob = "0.3"
dirs = "6"
toml = "1"
regex = "1"
textwrap = "0.16"
terminal_size = "0.4"
libsql = "0.9"
dotenvy = "0.15"
self_update = { version = "0.42", default-features = false, features = ["archive-tar", "compression-flate2", "rustls"] }
fs2 = "0.4"
rust-embed = { version = "8", features = ["interpolate-folder-path"] }
mime_guess = "2"
bollard = "0.20"
async-trait = "0.1"
openssl = { version = "0.10", optional = true }

# Observability
tracing = "0.1"
tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter", "json", "fmt"] }
tracing-appender = "0.2"
tracing-opentelemetry = { version = "0.28", optional = true }
opentelemetry = { version = "0.27", optional = true }
opentelemetry-otlp = { version = "0.27", optional = true }
opentelemetry_sdk = { version = "0.27", optional = true }

[features]
default = []
openssl-vendored = ["openssl/vendored"]
otlp = ["dep:tracing-opentelemetry", "dep:opentelemetry", "dep:opentelemetry-otlp", "dep:opentelemetry_sdk"]

[dev-dependencies]
tempfile = "3"
assert_cmd = "2"
predicates = "3"
tower = { version = "0.5", features = ["util"] }
http-body-util = "0.1"

[package]
name = "forge-common"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"
description = "Shared domain types for the Forge runtime platform"

[dependencies]
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
chrono = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde"] }
uuid = { version = "1", features = ["v4", "serde"] }
async-trait = "0.1"
anyhow = "1"
thiserror = "2"

1вҶ’// Copyright 2026 Forge Contributors
     2вҶ’// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
     3вҶ’//
     4вҶ’// Forge Runtime Platform вҖ” gRPC contract between forge CLI / Factory clients
     5вҶ’// and the forge-runtime daemon. Communicates over a Unix domain socket at
     6вҶ’// $FORGE_RUNTIME_DIR/forge.sock.
     7вҶ’//
     8вҶ’// Design reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-13-forge-runtime-platform-design.md
     9вҶ’
    10вҶ’syntax = "proto3";
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’package forge.runtime.v1;
    13вҶ’
    14вҶ’import "google/protobuf/timestamp.proto";
    15вҶ’import "google/protobuf/duration.proto";
    16вҶ’import "google/protobuf/struct.proto";
    17вҶ’
    18вҶ’option java_multiple_files = true;
    19вҶ’option java_package = "com.forge.runtime.v1";
    20вҶ’option go_package = "forge/runtime/v1;runtimev1";
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    23вҶ’// Service
    24вҶ’// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    25вҶ’
    26вҶ’// ForgeRuntime is the primary API exposed by the forge-runtime daemon.
    27вҶ’// CLI, Factory, and agent processes connect over a Unix domain socket.
    28вҶ’service ForgeRuntime {
    29вҶ’  // ---- Run lifecycle -------------------------------------------------------
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’  // Submit a new run for execution. The daemon validates policy, persists the
    32вҶ’  // run, and begins scheduling. Returns the initial RunInfo snapshot.
    33вҶ’  rpc SubmitRun(SubmitRunRequest) returns (RunInfo);
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’  // Attach to a running (or completed) run and receive a replay of events
    36вҶ’  // after `after_cursor`, followed by a live tail. Supports
    37вҶ’  // CLI disconnect/reconnect without state loss.
    38вҶ’  rpc AttachRun(AttachRunRequest) returns (stream RuntimeEvent);
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’  // Request graceful stop of a run. In-flight tasks receive SIGTERM; the
    41вҶ’  // daemon waits a grace period before force-killing.
    42вҶ’  rpc StopRun(StopRunRequest) returns (RunInfo);
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’  // Fetch the current snapshot of a run.
    45вҶ’  rpc GetRun(GetRunRequest) returns (RunInfo);
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’  // List runs, optionally filtered by project or status.
    48вҶ’  rpc ListRuns(ListRunsRequest) returns (ListRunsResponse);
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’  // ---- Task lifecycle ------------------------------------------------------
    51вҶ’
    52вҶ’  // Fetch a single task node.
    53вҶ’  rpc GetTask(GetTaskRequest) returns (TaskInfo);
    54вҶ’
    55вҶ’  // List task nodes within a run.
    56вҶ’  rpc ListTasks(ListTasksRequest) returns (ListTasksResponse);
    57вҶ’
    58вҶ’  // Stream a task-scoped filtered projection of output chunks from the
    59вҶ’  // authoritative runtime event log.
    60вҶ’  rpc StreamTaskOutput(StreamTaskOutputRequest) returns (stream TaskOutputEvent);
    61вҶ’
    62вҶ’  // Create a child task node under an existing parent. The daemon runs the
    63вҶ’  // request through the policy engine and may gate on approval.
    64вҶ’  rpc CreateChildTask(CreateChildTaskRequest) returns (CreateChildTaskResponse);
    65вҶ’
    66вҶ’  // Terminate a task. The daemon sends SIGTERM to the agent, waits a grace
    67вҶ’  // period, then SIGKILL.
    68вҶ’  rpc KillTask(KillTaskRequest) returns (KillTaskResponse);
    69вҶ’
    70вҶ’  // ---- Approval flow -------------------------------------------------------
    71вҶ’
    72вҶ’  // Stream pending approval requests. The daemon pushes new requests as they
    73вҶ’  // arise; the client resolves them via ResolveApproval.
    74вҶ’  rpc PendingApprovals(PendingApprovalsRequest) returns (stream ApprovalRequest);
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’  // Approve or deny a pending approval request.
    77вҶ’  rpc ResolveApproval(ResolveApprovalRequest) returns (ResolveApprovalResponse);
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’  // ---- MCP service management ----------------------------------------------
    80вҶ’
